speaker: MATT
raw_text: Hey, everyone. Sorry about that little issue there. But thank you for your patience. I appreciate that. Going back over what had transpired previously. So the party was hired to go into Kraghammer seeking Lady Kima of Vord, who is a renowned folk hero, a halfling paladin of much renown, following Bahamut, the Platinum Dragon of justice and good, who went on a vision quest, apparently seeing some sort of evil brewing beneath Kraghammer, and went on her own to seek it and destroy it. Hasn't been seen for many weeks-- months, even -- since. And so the party has then gone into Kraghammer to attempt to locate her. After some conversing, some brawling, some interesting circumstances, they found information that she disappeared into the Mithril mines just underneath the lower circle of Kraghammer. They've since battled a few creatures, apparently these strange monstrosities are coming out of the mines recently. And they've had dwarven miners being taken and disappearing. They charged into the mines, found one of the series of tunnels that had broken into the mines from some other source, encountered a few umber hulks, were ambushed by some duergar, which are subterranean, dark, ashen grey--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point? It's at some point in the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll rescue her! And we're going to kill your god! (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I did it, I did it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: An ichor spray comes across.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ugh!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was the worst thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The most awkward throat-slitting I've ever heard of.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That was very fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to kill your god. Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck it, just go with it! (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Commit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the body goes limp in your hands, you kinda let it tumble over the waterfall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good times. Good times, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Um, now you guys are standing over Grog in this kind of makeshift--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a little sticky, slimy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- camp site. The embers of the fire kinda going low.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm starved.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, she's researching--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we keep-- I'm going to keep watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Absolutely.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: With Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We need to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do we have any idea what time it is?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You go ahead and you eventually find one of the corpses of the umber hulk. It takes you a good 25 minutes to eventually carve through its relatively thick hide, chitinous exterior. You put the head on a makeshift spike, and it is now there on display.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm glad we killed them before they used that on us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, it's pretty standard.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's a pew pew stick.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pretty standard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a pew pew stick!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Would anybody like to tinker? I mean, I learned this when I was 12. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, you're unconscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'll hold onto it--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, yeah. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think Grog may have soiled himself a little. We should probably get Keyleth on the books.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Take a nap so that, maybe--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Make sure everyone's in and close by, and get done with everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, she's gonna--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, you need to study, so we'll take first watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm gonna take you guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's true, you should study while we watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You can't study and watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm gonna cast Anti-Life Shell. That way everyone at least gets one good hour of sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good! I like it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's take a nap.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

It would take a while for you to actually hack it out of the side. It can be done, but it will take you a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And none of that red light is lighting any of this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not in the center of the village. Those are only towards the outside walls of the cavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So we're in the dark.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I go up and see if I can get one of those little red rocks?

raw_text: All right. So now, Tiberius, Vax, you guys walk across the bridge, keeping grip both side rails, feeling it rock to and fro from the continuous warm bellowing of wind up from the cavern beneath you. Eventually you bring your way across. What looks like you should be stepping on terrain is still the bridge, and it takes you a minute to adjust to the fact that you're still going across the bridge, but it looks like rocky terrain. Eventually you make it to the other side and you grab purchase on stone. Looking ahead of you, you can see about 60 feet ahead of you the outskirts of the war camp.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Cool. So you go ahead and you cast Hallucinatory Terrain--

preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.text: Thank you!

preceding_turns.text: And you can actually see that the bridge looks attached to it, it's a very, very convincing illusion. And you kind of have to remind yourself that it's an illusion, just so you don't fall for it either!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, be careful guys, please be careful!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So you're kind of chilling in the middle of the bridge?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. The rest of you are--

preceding_turns.text: -- on the other side-- outside of the bridge.

preceding_turns.text: I'm out with them.

preceding_turns.text: Okay. So you're off the bridge now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Gone. Yeah.

preceding_turns.text: I loved that carpet, damn it.

preceding_turns.text: Who wants to have a picnic?

preceding_turns.text: I mean, seriously.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So how many (monster sounds) are over there ?

preceding_turns.text: You only saw the one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And how many duergar left? How many--

preceding_turns.text: After they fell into--

preceding_turns.text: After all that, you saw a few more kind of filter out from the town, you have probably about 25 visible at that moment that have all backed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to hang on him visually and I'm gonna wait to take another shot until he tries to stand up again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So if he tries to stand up again, I want to shoot him again.

preceding_turns.text: But wait, you can shoot him while he's on the ground! Can't you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can shoot him, but it's at disadvantage to shoot someone who's prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's on the ground?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Technically, he's got cover. And it's funnier to just wait.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Pike left back there to finish up the renovation of the temple of Sarenrae that was discovered. The rest of the party moved on back to Emon, the rest of their business complete. Within Emon, they returned to Greyskull Keep, where they encountered a fan, apparently, of Vox Machina, that had been camping for two weeks outside, who was promptly bludgeoned in the temple by Vax to teach him a lesson. Brought back from the brink, and taught that hard lesson-- that this is a dangerous field-- managed to seemingly reach out and touch the heart of this young, intrepid, wannabe adventurer to possibly try and better himself before he throws himself into danger's way. And he ran off to train harder.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good to know. Well, fantastic. Do you have any announcements you want to make, Sam, or shall we jump in?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. Just that I don't have any dice but I can use Orion's and they're going to be awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have so many dice. Take this giant bag and just fish some out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great. Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The Bag of Disagreeable Dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, let him find them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, then in just a minute, let us begin this next episode of Critical Role. [dramatic music]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. All right, so to give you guys a little heads-up of what has recently transpired in the story. The party, after completing their business in the city of Vasselheim, on the far-off northwest continent. Grog, of course, having an epic battle in the Crucible fight pit against Kern, reclaiming his honor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) I am the champion, my friends.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Indeed. Upon returning to the keep, notice was sent off to one of the other members of the Council of Tal'Dorei, Seeker Asum, who is the halfling head of whispers and all sorts of subterfuge across the countryside, who came to you notifying you that within a week's time, the Briarwoods, Lord and Lady Briarwood, are supposed to be arriving here within Emon for a feast to discuss building a bridge and a more political joining between Emon, the Tal'Dorei royal family, and these up-and-coming lords and ladies, who have a very dark history with Percy's character. You've been asked to join as part of this feast as you previously requested with sovereign Tal'Dorei, that should anything involved the Briarwoods that you be included. You have a week to figure out what you want to do in advance of that. And that is where we left off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) I am the champion, my friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike left back there to finish up the renovation of the temple of Sarenrae that was discovered. The rest of the party moved on back to Emon, the rest of their business complete. Within Emon, they returned to Greyskull Keep, where they encountered a fan, apparently, of Vox Machina, that had been camping for two weeks outside, who was promptly bludgeoned in the temple by Vax to teach him a lesson. Brought back from the brink, and taught that hard lesson-- that this is a dangerous field-- managed to seemingly reach out and touch the heart of this young, intrepid, wannabe adventurer to possibly try and better himself before he throws himself into danger's way. And he ran off to train harder.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Dagger, dagger, hug.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Indeed. Upon returning to the keep, notice was sent off to one of the other members of the Council of Tal'Dorei, Seeker Asum, who is the halfling head of whispers and all sorts of subterfuge across the countryside, who came to you notifying you that within a week's time, the Briarwoods, Lord and Lady Briarwood, are supposed to be arriving here within Emon for a feast to discuss building a bridge and a more political joining between Emon, the Tal'Dorei royal family, and these up-and-coming lords and ladies, who have a very dark history with Percy's character. You've been asked to join as part of this feast as you previously requested with sovereign Tal'Dorei, that should anything involved the Briarwoods that you be included. You have a week to figure out what you want to do in advance of that. And that is where we left off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) I am the champion, my friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike left back there to finish up the renovation of the temple of Sarenrae that was discovered. The rest of the party moved on back to Emon, the rest of their business complete. Within Emon, they returned to Greyskull Keep, where they encountered a fan, apparently, of Vox Machina, that had been camping for two weeks outside, who was promptly bludgeoned in the temple by Vax to teach him a lesson. Brought back from the brink, and taught that hard lesson-- that this is a dangerous field-- managed to seemingly reach out and touch the heart of this young, intrepid, wannabe adventurer to possibly try and better himself before he throws himself into danger's way. And he ran off to train harder.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Dagger, dagger, hug.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Right now, after this discussion, you guys' arrival, I'd say it's probably mid-afternoon. First things first, though, after this discussion with Seeker Asum, who leaves the keep, you do have a brief moment of awkward silence as most of the people that work within your keep turn to you. Laina looks about the rest of you and goes, "By the way, we've been working here for about six weeks on the pay of four. We're coming close to our next pay scheme, and you tend to leave often and for extended periods of time. We would also like to be paid, if possible."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What time of the day is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'd say it'd put you there about the same pace, about two days on eagle, because you can just go straight through. I mean, you want to take the road because you know that there's a lot of dangerous things that wander the wild that are between there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Calm down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can forge paperwork, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes, he can. He's actually the guy we go to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He is that guy you meet on the corner of Sandy Alley.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can make us all into birds.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are we supposed to go meet with the council right away?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, in a week.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What do we do in the week? Prepare. Recon?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can do things. How long of a ride is it to Kraghammer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To Kraghammer, on horseback? It's about two and a half days.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Solo style, if you're fast, and back?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fast and back? I'd say just under two days, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How about the giant eagle flies?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what if you're flying?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'd say, well, the horse is probably a little bit faster than the eagle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But you don't have to stick to the road as an eagle.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No. I'm the clerk. I run the front of the-- follow me." And he gets up and leads you further into the structure to the central circular room, the white-marbled room that contains all the doors around, and in the center there is the Traverse Junction. This is the place you guys have stumbled into every single time you've teleported back to Emon. And there you see the elven wizard who you recently bothered naught but maybe five hours ago, who is currently setting up some sheets, comes back. "Oh. Hello, again."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, hi, if you do not remember me, I am Tiberius Stormwind, from Draconia. Moreover, Vox Machina, over at Greyskull Keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Where have I heard that--"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Well, we told you multiple times before. We met with you using your sigil of teleport-- it's fine. I've come here on a matter of business anyway, so that's neither here nor there. I was wondering if I may procure an address, or number as it were, for a sigil to Kraghammer. I believe the Thunderbrands would have some facility near theirs or on their own premises where their sigil could be channeled through the Circle of Teleportation spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you're talking about sigils for teleportation, you would probably have to discuss that with Gatekeeper Xanthas."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Where is he? I thought that was you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, then I will-- just one moment." And he reaches into a pouch and pulls out this mirror, this oval mirror and sets it onto his desk. Reaches underneath and pulls out a small chest. Opens up the chest, and you can see in this chest that there are probably two dozen of these tiny little marbles, all different colors, some different shapes, and he goes, these little labels you can't really see from your perspective. He takes one out, places it in the top of the mirror, and as he does, the mirror all of a sudden shimmers for a second, and what was originally a normal reflection is now this light, glowing blue, slightly glowing water-like surface. He takes it and lifts it up to himself. "Hello. Hello? Hello!" A moment later, you hear this distant voice echoing with this strange warbling filter over it. "I'm coming, what's taking your time. Yes, Headmaster, what do you require?" "Well, there's a gentleman here, dragonborn, who is requesting use of your sigil of teleportation. Do you know this gentleman?" Turns it around, and you can see in there, this is the same long-bearded dwarven individual that met you at the actual Thunderbrand house, and had a bit of an argument with you early in your travels in Kraghammer.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hello.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm Tiberius Stormwind from Draconia.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Moreover, Vox Machina over at Greyskull Keep here in Emon. We are the saviors of this town and whatnot. Anyway, that's neither here nor there. I went through this fellow, who I thought was the job of this fellow, but it wasn't either of those jobs, it was you who I've been needing to talk to, I'm assuming?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What do you require from me, then?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm hoping to procure an address or access to the sigils of Kraghammer through my Circle of Teleportation spell and whatnot using your arcane gates. That's about it. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well put!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you're asking for access to one of our closely guarded sigils for the Thunderbrands, you say, within Kraghammer?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, yes. We'll have to speak with them as well.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As far as time goes, you haven't quite gotten to that point yet, for these guys. You get to Gilmore's. You enter the interior. You can see that for the most part, there aren't a lot of people in here. It's getting later in the day, and a lot of the customer business seems to be happening almost earlier, either pre-noon, or around noon. There's no current sign of Gilmore. You do see, across the way-- what's her name, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait. No?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Good try!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can bring-- oh, we're not there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The headmaster takes the marble out and says, "I'm sorry that didn't seem to go your way."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It's fine, Headmaster. I'll be back in about 15 minutes with the proper proof that we did procure the things that the man, that needed to have proof, did. Excuse me! And I get the hell out of there. And as soon as I get out of there, I cast Fly on myself, and I Gandalf (whoosh) right back to the keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. You're eating. You're crafting. Are you going with the rest of them to Gilmore?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. I guess so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys make your way to the outside of Gilmore's Glorious Goods.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As we walk, do we see Tiberius fly over our heads?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Actually, I'm staying at the Keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Well, Travis is my target, right? Are you still at the Keep?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm eating.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few minutes go by. Sherri comes back. She brings two books over. "Best I can tell, these may be what you're looking for? I can't quite--" She sets them down. One is a book that is the properties of various liquids, chemicals, and things that are found on this plane and otherwise. It's a good way to locate and identify phantom liquids, should you come across any.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's all right. I understand  people grow busy. There are things you must tend to, and that's all right. And I appreciate you being so honest, I really do."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: In addition to that, I am changed, and promises mean more to me, even more than they did before. And I promise to follow through. In the interest of time for the game, DM, Vax tells the whole story of the Underdark, of the horn, of how close it was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ugh, what is taking him so long, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While that's happening, the rest of you are left there awkwardly staring about.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did Sherri find my book? Sherri?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "That? Yes, we do have a couple of those. Hold on." She darts up the stairway in the other room and goes to the second floor. A few moments later, she comes back down with these two small chests-- they're about that big, each. One's a lighter wood color, the other is painted black all over. "We have both of these. They should shield something of this size from most low-level magical detections. These will run you about 200 gold apiece."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I spoke too soon!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So perfect! So perfect for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She reaches out and takes the books and slides them back, and says, "Well, if that's how you feel, perhaps these can be returned to those university students that perhaps left them by. I should put these in the lost and found."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sherri!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sherri. If I may. What Keyleth was trying to say was that she never got a chance to go to college, and that she wanted to get that university experience.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good luck."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, that didn't work. Hey, can you serve me, now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Scanlan. Scanlan! Will you buy those books for me, and I'll reimburse you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can hear you. I'm right here." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Walk away!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, I'm happy to help you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was wondering if you had a small box of some sort that could carry a magical item, and perhaps shield it from detection.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "The deal was since you did not have funds, and you had to retrieve this money from this 'Dwarf Town', as you so graciously put it, that you would borrow the money, at the time, and then pay me back within a month. Within a month."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm pretty magic-stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Then don't be the one that does it. Talk to your friend, this Tiberius fellow. He'll know what to do, I'm sure."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Correct. One last. It's small or it's boring, it's business. I need a dagger. I don't want one for free or anything; I want to purchase it, but we're a little low on funds. The reason Tiberius needs to go to Kraghammer is because we're owed a large sum of money.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, you mentioned this last time, when you almost opened a line of Gilmore's Glorious Credit."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hm. I'm not there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What would the credit entail?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Previously, we discussed 25,000 gold pieces, the amount of which you were going to retrieve."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was going to-- what was that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Retrieve."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Retrieve? God, I've been in the Underdark so long. What was the deal?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Fly is a concentration spell. You cast it again. Your amulet keeps it up for two rounds, which is 12 seconds, and then the previous Fly spell deteriorates.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Brilliant!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that gives me a wonderful idea! And then, I'll cast it at 4th level again, and I'll go (pew-pew!) and cast it on--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just because I think it's funny-- Grog is flying over too, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm in that kitchen, floating around, farting and eating food. Look, I've got priorities.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, well, I see you guys are flying? The two of you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you fly down and cast Fly on the rest of them?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Right. Come on, follow me!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit, I've never flown before! This is amazing!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm flying, too? What's happening?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are now floating. You guys are floating, as well. You're coasting over Emon for roughly 12 or so seconds before Grog, you drop to the ground, and now Tiberius and Vex, you begin to plummet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why are we plummeting?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Two notes. There is no sigil in Vasselheim. They have been outlawed and destroyed to prevent arcane intrusion or subterfuge. There have been, historically, many a war's passed in which Vasselheim nearly fell to the sudden appearance of small armies within its walls, and as such, many of these sigils, all of them have been destroyed over the past 1,000 years. However, it was the third war--" And he's going into a long diatribe about the history of Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful. That makes me quite happy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To Kraghammer, was it?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, but I do not want to use that favor for that sigil. I would like to call our friends, and I brought the item. I bring out the amulet. I want to show our friends in Kraghammer that we actually brought them the thing to get their permission to go on their sigil, and the other continent that we were on is called what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Vasselheim?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes. The favor I would like for Gilmore is to get the Vasselheim sigil, please. But let's contact our friends at Kraghammer first.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point in time, with all the travel back and forth, it's getting close to dusk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Persuasion-wise.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you guys head back to the Keep, you meet up with Grog and Percy, what about you guys? What're you doing? For, Tiberius, Scanlan, and Vex.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We go through.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's just the sigil. You have to cast the spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Is that what you want to do? Is that what's going on?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. No, actually, I need to sleep. All that flying left me a bit exhausted.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sorcerers, they improvise.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, you're saying that we cannot go through?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Well, hold on. Maybe there's a way I could trigger the spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go home and go to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Let's get a drink, maybe? It's only been a day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You can cast that sigil anywhere, right? You don't have to be here? We could do it from home.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then let's just fucking go home.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What time of the day is this? It's still afternoonish, right?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "All right. It would take me about two days? And you've already supplied the material, other than a bit of the side bits. I'll say I'll charge you 100 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I'm Tiberius Stormwind from Draconia; moreover, from Vox Machina--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What can I help you with?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Sorry, right. We've done business before. I would like to commission you. And as I'm doing this I'm taking out five of my daggers that I have, and I'm going to hand them to her. I would like to commission you to somehow craft these daggers into a type of bended glaive, if you would. I don't know how long it would take you to do so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You see, it's like, it would look like this, but it's--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Krull?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, totally, but the blades are bended, but they're all connected in the center. You could use an iron, or a steel ingot to connect the--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can certainly try, yeah, aye."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Certainly. Thank you. All right, it should be done within the next two days. You're free to come by and check on it if you like, but it'll be done."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You see, it's like, it would look like this, but it's--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Krull?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, totally, but the blades are bended, but they're all connected in the center. You could use an iron, or a steel ingot to connect the--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can certainly try, yeah, aye."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. It would take me about two days? And you've already supplied the material, other than a bit of the side bits. I'll say I'll charge you 100 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: 100 gold pieces?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Aye, I'm a specialist!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You went to the good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What, that's all? I mean, oh yes, of course. Here's 120.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It will take you about, from where you are right now, 30 minutes. All right, so you guys get back to the keep. Grog has eaten his fill.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm totally down an alleyway, through the alley, onto another main thoroughfare. I grab a random person on the street. Excuse me, I'm looking for a specific butcher. Harold the butcher. Do you know? I think he's in the upper slums. Can you point me toward him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The individual you stopped is a younger gentleman, probably in his mid-20s or so, a bit dirty in the face and coming from a day of work, going to the local tavern to finish his eve with some drinks. "I can't say I do. I mean, there are butchers all over town. There's one--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where's the upper slums? Upper slums, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, that would be north side of Emon. That way." He points you towards the--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't even say anything else. I just walk. I don't know how long that will take me.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A couple moments later, a voice comes from the other side. "Hello?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You take a little bit of time in peering over the materials, looking at the bed size, looking at the boot size, looking at whatever you can nearby. This bed is definitely weathered. It's been used for a long time. There are a couple of small knick-knacks and toys that are discarded underneath that have been there for a while that lead you to believe that this was at one point a boy's room. However, what you do notice is that most of the materials, most of the belongings, anything that you would normally see in a boy's room, anything that would say that a person lives here seem to have been hastily grabbed and taken off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And the father went out the other way? All right, so I creep back out the window and I go out the front and I knock really loud on the front door.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That was in character. All right, cool. Let's get back and see if we can, see if we can roll through some of this stuff. As you wake the next morning, you guys awaken to the dull sound of metal clanging as Percy has already been the first to wake and is back in the workshop. Tiberius, you're well-rested. Everyone's spells have returned. You get to make a beard roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, from our very strict, very strict five-minute break. We're very good at that. Yeah. That's right. Couple quick things. First off, we just hit 7,500 subscribers! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's amazing for many reasons. But all of you who are currently watching, be active in the chatroom so you can be included in the next giveaway we're doing. Giveaway's going to include a signed picture of the cast, a signed poster by the cast of the amazing art of our fantastic artist, Kit Buss.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Maybe signed by Kit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. So please be active in the chatroom, be part of that giveaway when it gets put out, and thank you guys so much for being so amazing and getting us to that point that's--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! You 7,500 people, fucking thank you, man. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But only you 7,500. The rest can fuck off.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's not going to matter. You keep talking, and as you're explaining this, he's not even paying attention to you. The dwarf is staring at the amulet, looking at the back of it where the inscription is. And you swear for a minute, you see what looks like a tear, gathering in the corner of the eye of the dwarf, who then reaches up and wipes it from the corner and takes the amulet and puts it in the pocket and says, "My apologies. Thank you for returning this. You were talking about the carpet, right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So you come into my home and try to extort me?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No, not at all! Here's what happened, see. We were on an adventure-- I pull out the carpet out of my Bag of Holding. And this carpet was enchanted once with the ability to fly, and it helped us many times in many adventures. It was de-chanted, I guess, if you want to say it. And we need it re-chanted. And I thought that House Thunderbrand would be a strong enough house to have the ability to do such a thing. Maybe not as it seems. And I hand him the amulet as I'm saying this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He takes the amulet.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And not as a trade, but as a favor for just the effort, or maybe because I'm not strong enough as a sorcerer myself to do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: People speak very, very highly of your abilities. Can I assist him?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We're looking at the better part of a year to enchant this and that'll cost you about, time included, that'll cost 50,000 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You want it to be enchanted?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, we've heard very wonderful things about your abilities and not many people can do such a feat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We have done a carpet before. Only one. It's very possible."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did it look anything like this one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Not this pattern, no, that one was more of a blue. I mean this can be enchanted, but the materials and the time put into it-- It's a very pricey endeavor. A carpet of this size, especially."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How much?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 11? "Looking at your number, I can see that you've certainly encountered some dangerous things in your travels, taken some losses. And I will say, since you've left, we haven't had at least one excursion interrupting our mine, which has been very helpful. The fact that you've returned with these tales-- I believe most of it. I'd say, given that circumstance, I'd be happy to pay you up to 18,000 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There were these wrinkled testicles that were walking around on feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He almost died! Just from this one little brain.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Strange thing. If you're stupid, and it looks at you with its wrinkled testicle face? You go unconscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I know the creatures you speak of. I've dealt with my share of duergar, though--"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I completely stayed away from it, because I was terrified.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Roll high!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we all rolling? Because we're all talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You were spearheading that part of the group.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I was? I thought Scanlan was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan already made his roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What about giving her advantage because I was talking about testicles?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No testicle advantage on that roll. I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: In what circumstance would testicle advantage come into play?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: At Gilmore's.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That would be 11.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He looks back at you, very slowly, as you do this, with a tense stare. (laughs) "All right, all right." And he starts bringing up coin purse, after coin purse, after coin purse, counting it up as he does. It takes him about five minutes or so, and eventually he counts out, at your vision to improve it, 25,000 gold pieces and platinum pieces and pushes it across to you guys. You've retrieved that. No additional funds, as no scalps were collected, but you do get 25,000.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't have my hand cone out. 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Money!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, what are you suggesting?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to suggest to him that he is an honorable man and we had a deal. And that he should honor it, to the fullest extent of the word.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks about at the rest of you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I also, as a cantrip-- oh god, I hope it's not concentration. Forget it. I'm not going to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Greyspine name holds weight in this city, the last thing I want is that that be sullied by miscommunication. If we agreed to 25,000, I'll pay 25,000. But I hear a single disagreement out there, or a bad word that came from your lips about our exchange here, and I'm going to come for the rest of that money."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I hold up the duergar's severed arm, and I curl all the fingers in and make a thumbs-up like that.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes about ten minutes before the keep doors open and the rest of your party returns.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth has been weaving flowers in, and I have been knotting braids. Started at the head, and then worked all the way down Trinket's back and down the tail. The entire time. Feverishly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I come up for lunch. Keyleth, can I borrow you for a-- what in good god?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it important? Do you need me right now? This is pretty important.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is Trinket comfortable with this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's so comfortable! Look, I gave him plenty of treats, he doesn't even know what's happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (sad bear noises) This mournful low, guttural groan. You're pretty sure it translates to, "Why?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I feed him a piece of chocolate at that moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Don't give him chocolate!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He licks it off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, can I borrow you? When you have a moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ten more minutes. I'll be down in ten minutes.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No, they're arriving now in six days and arriving for the actual feast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, to start a charge? Then you can do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah, I was going to Frankenstein this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. We can begin the roll now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a tinkering roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's better. 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 20, okay. Not actually doing the charge yet, because you're building the basis of the structure. You help him downstairs in his workshop with the various materials to begin crafting the actual shell of the the item. What else are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go do recon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what are we checking out, though? I mean, it's not like the Briarwoods are out in public. We don't know anything about them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to ask around town about the Briarwoods.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We need to get into the Briarwoods' estate and talk to them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, they're not local. They're coming into town. They're coming into town in five to seven days.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The crowing sound of a distant rooster. The farmlands that surround the outside of Emon, which are relatively close, within earshot. The morning sun rises, cresting over the eastern skyline of Tal'Dorei. Grog, you come to consciousness, stretch, reach up to rub-- half a beard.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a stealth check. Make a perception check. You just broke a mechanical pencil! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: DM? I rolled a natural 20. I have advantage on stealth checks, and I rolled an 18 and a 20. Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sleeping soundly, drunk, you do not hear the approach of Vax. As you pull out your blade and get ready, I want you to roll a sleight of hand check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: After the 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One thing to not be noticed. It's another to shave someone's face without being noticed. This is about stillness of fingers, not just how stealthy you can be.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm using Luck. Okay, so I rolled a 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll a perception check, Grog. With advantage because this is against your face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why are you so evil?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why did he trip me and make me eat shit two months ago?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Appetizer of things to come, my friend.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so over the next few days, you've constructed what amounts to a large, metallic gauntlet. You have taken this gauntlet and wired all through it insulation and metallic insulating tips in what is essentially a small, for lack of a better term, alternator of some kind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He is dumb as a post. Why does he know--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Because you're the only fucking idiot stupid enough to do that!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Magic is mysterious! Why couldn't you grow half a beard due to magic?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe I don't know. Let's say I assume. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: However, it is a new morning. Roll a beard check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Eight, four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, big guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so as a note, Percy, I want you to make four more tinkering rolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to give him inspiration for one of them. (singing) Do you want to build a machine gun?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Why, yes, I really do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What have you got?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 28, okay. Second one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Dice going everywhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Inspiration, d10.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I got a d6 from the last one you did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: d6 is what I gave you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: d10, so I'll roll a d10 with the next one. That one was 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Third one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, and the fourth one?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, you suffer 14 points of lightning damage and are stunned for a round. I need you to go ahead and roll a d20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did it work? Try it on Tiberius.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Touch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Tiberius, make a constitution saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No! (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. To hide and to speak. To stay did not stick, unfortunately. You had two rolls that were high enough to get those two in there though. Hide and speak. All right. The morning has come to fruition. You have a few hours before you're supposed to arrive at the palace. You have been notified that this is a very formal event.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Circus bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll go over the logistics of that some other time, but Trinket has now learned a roly-poly trample attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we call it the roly-poly or the cannonball?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's your call.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's your call. Call it what you want.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Roly-poly is cute; cannonball is--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think I like cannonball because it's better to shout during the battle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Trinket cannonball.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Roly-poly!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: For brevity, I rolled six dice. I rolled, the only high ones worth mentioning was a 13, a 15, and 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With a 15 and an 18--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: 17, I mean.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And the, because they're all negative three. And I attempted to teach to Lockheed to hide and speak and to stay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, dressed up, you all make your way to the Cloudtop district as the sun begins to bring itself to the other side of the horizon. As you approach the outside of the Tal'Dorei palace, there are general guards set up to accept guests. They're just there in wait as part of their process today. As soon as you arrive and announce yourselves as having arrived, is there anything you want to do to prepare?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The dust tends to glisten, and where there was once charcoal outline, a mirror to your once beautiful beard continues, full, as if nothing had happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach into the bag of holding and I take out a little goblet, and I pour some ale in it. And I walk over to Vax, and I go, here you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This looks very good. Thank you, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're welcome. Would you like some more?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I don't want to cloud my thinking before we go up against the Briarwoods.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Probably smart. I'll take that back. I crush the goblet in my hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This creaking metal sound. The goblet is now destroyed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh no! What happened?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He leads you into the green meeting room you guys have had previous council meetings with. It's currently empty. He sets the guards to the side and brings you inside and says, "All right, so if my little birds speak truth, they should be arriving around dusk, so we probably have no more than 20 minutes before they are here themselves. If you're not aware, they should be referred to as Lord and Lady Briarwood. Their names: Lord Sylas Briarwood and Lady Delilah Briarwood."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you approach, you make yourselves known. The guards lead you to the front area of the palace. The sun's just starting to hit the distant side of the water of the Ozmit Sea to the west of Emon. As you guys finish talking to the front guard that you've arrived, Seeker Asum steps out of the front of the palace and sees you. "Very good. Glad you could make it. Please enter. I will wish to speak with you briefly before the event this evening."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Are you missing anyone?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yes. Percy wasn't feeling very well. He ended up staying home.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. That's unfortunate. His skills could have been of use to me. Come inside."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I pat Vax's hand-- Percy's hand. What the fuck. This is creepy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Call him Vaxy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Vaxy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That will work just fine. Thank you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, Vax, if you can hear me, follow me when the meal begins. I may have use for you. All right, so. The Briarwoods have a very good relationship with Uriel and the Tal'Dorei council, established back before they expanded outside of Wildemount. I've sent two of my best gatherers into Wynandir to seek out more information on them. I don't know when that information will be available; they've only been gone but three days, and hopefully in time, we'll learn more of what their machinations may be. Many of the noble houses of Whitestone were left vacant when the de Rolos passed and were given to friends of the Briarwoods. Whispers that reach me speak of these new nobles, not being highborn, nor affiliated with the gentry at all, so I'm curious as to what allies they've made either before or after taking Whitestone for themselves."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He also has a bit of a cold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I wish to observe the Briarwoods myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (gritty voice) I wish to observe.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You've got to go more nasal, Taliesin.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, nasal.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you need to go down here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sort of like sandpaper on a chalkboard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I should have done something about this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Asum goes, "All right. If we are to do this, I would appreciate a little bit of honesty at the front point."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm Tiberius Stormwind from Draconia.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is not Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Asum, it's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I gathered as much, as soon as you began to speak. Now, I can understand, based on our previous conversation, that you have interest in keeping low key, but know that I am an ally in this. If you're not here, where is the rest of your party. Where is Vax?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Vax is here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's stealthing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He's being quiet.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "The only thing I can recall as the information of the Briarwoods, at least what's come this way was while they were ruling the lands in Wildemount as part of their kingdom there, Lady Delilah Briarwood was an accomplished practitioner of the arcane arts, a long-trained wizard as far as I know and been through quite a few different schools and had a brief stint here in Emon training at the Lyceum, but that was naught but nine, ten years back? Maybe more."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We will be as quiet as a house mouse. However, if you need us to act, just utter the word "jenga."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Jenga."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I also make a matching corsage and pin it on his lapel, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you do, he looks down at it and goes, "Thank you; it's not my color."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, that's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I appreciate the offer but my theme goes with browns and blacks so as not to be seen. This is a bit bright for my taste."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It might have had a more utilitarian purpose, but it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At which point, one of the other council members goes, "Um." and puts his hand out. "It's all right," and he pats the hand of his wife. He looks over at you with a smile. "We know not if it is connected with the disease, but before all this occurred, we became very close friends with the de Rolo family about ten or so years ago. Actually, the first time we met is an interesting tale, if you want to tell it, please." His wife goes, "All right all right, so they were taking a brief vacation east to Wildemount. Apparently, they had not traveled and they brought their family with them, a whole gaggle of small children. They were coming this direction. Now our homestead is on the western outskirts of Wildemount so one of the first places you traverse before going into the kingdom proper. However, the family, the de Rolo family had been caught in a bad downpour, a storm that had blown in and made travel rather impossible, and apparently they stopped not far from our homestead. They came to our front door asking for shelter for the evening, and we were happy to house them. We gave them dry clothes, food for the night, and well, a rather fast friendship was forged. As we quickly began to notice they weren't just rabble of the street, they themselves were highborn as we were and in a very dire circumstance. So we let them in, they stayed for but a week. We showed them about the western side of Wildemount and let them on their way. And since then we've had many different years we've spent together as families going on various journeys and trips together. But as time went on we got busy on our own and separate ways and we hadn't spoken in a while. They were in the process of discussing the building of the Uriel bridge about five years ago, and we were going to meet with them about pursuing this venture as a joint-- well, when the disease took them. The family passed on. We were surprised that they would've considered leaving Whitestone in our name, and we couldn't say no, at this point. To carry on their legacy, I felt, was important enough let alone the memory of that to carry on the building of this Uriel bridge. I thought I really wanted to spearhead the wishes of Frederick."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, actually. Dr. Ripley is one of our esteemed physicians in Whitestone. Yes, recently come into some land herself, although she has been on holiday for some time. Following her own research as doctors are wont to do."

preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I want to train a little, want to work out a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Totally. I understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep. I need a target, though. Hey, can you call one of those little ghostly guys over?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can have five of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, right, fine. The first one of you that brings me a cow wins.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do. And you take a few minutes to figure out the pitch and wobble of it, and it's less of a directional flight, more than it is a combination of how you hold the front of the broom and concentrate on its direction. But nevertheless, a few moments pass, and you get the hang of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yay, this is awesome! I'm really high!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I instruct the band to start playing Flight of the Bumblebee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whistling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh! Can I take my hands off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do, and you start going, "Whoa!" Go ahead and make a dexterity check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my God. I probably should have been lower to the ground when I said that. Okay, that's fine. Oh, I'm still exhausted!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's fine. You're fine. A really bad roll might have had a circumstance, but you manage to catch yourself on the broom in time and, okay. You're getting the hang of it. It's still not something you're used to, but it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I point it down towards the ground and get lower?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do. And you take a few minutes to figure out the pitch and wobble of it, and it's less of a directional flight, more than it is a combination of how you hold the front of the broom and concentrate on its direction. But nevertheless, a few moments pass, and you get the hang of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yay, this is awesome! I'm really high!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I instruct the band to start playing Flight of the Bumblebee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whistling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh! Can I take my hands off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do, and you start going, "Whoa!" Go ahead and make a dexterity check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my God. I probably should have been lower to the ground when I said that. Okay, that's fine. Oh, I'm still exhausted!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's fine. You're fine. A really bad roll might have had a circumstance, but you manage to catch yourself on the broom in time and, okay. You're getting the hang of it. It's still not something you're used to, but it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I point it down towards the ground and get lower?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You all eventually find your ways to your rooms, happy and comfortable in the fact that no one has to take watch for this night. As you all get to bed, a few hours into the evening, Keyleth, you are just starting to doze off when you hear a knock at the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, guys, it didn't work, you can go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all get up. The music stops. And they all slowly drift out of the room, one by one. Fireplace still crackling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's nice in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm sitting wherever there's a lounge of any kind. I'm working in the sketchbook. Working on things.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not a worry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll be reading my alchemy book, because I have had so little time to catch up on my reading.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We never talk anymore, do we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We never do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Mmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so happy! It's the best!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So good!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try to sleep. It's going to be really, really hard because I'm so excited, but I really need to get some rest!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did you feed the dog?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Today? I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, anyone want to do anything else? You going to go rest for the evening?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm good.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He goes over and takes one of the nearby chairs and sets it down, places one across from him for you to sit, gestures with his one arm. Sits down. "I would not speak with those I do not trust. And I have seen your purity of soul, Keyleth. So I come to you with these concerns." His eyes trail off for a moment in the distance, and he gathers his thoughts and his words. "Four years ago, a young woman came to Pyrah for redemption. A lost girl, cast out and wishing for acceptance. We, as we do with outcasts, took her in, taught her. And in time, called her one of our own, one of the Pyrah. In recent months, she began to grow distant, taking to staying in the Cindergrove unattended for days at a time. One afternoon, the mountain moved, angry with shifting fires. We felt a disturbance in the Gate, and when we went to investigate, there she was, commanding powers we did not know she had."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps awake) Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You open the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: On my door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On your door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Keyleth, it is Cerkonos. Please open up. I wish to speak with you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I open the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Cerkonos comes in. The first thing you notice-- previously, you had seen what was his burned-off arm at the shoulder. You can now see the flesh is extended and is currently very slowly in the process of regenerating his lost arm. And it catches you off-guard for a moment, and he puts his hand up and says, "Sorry, it is not very presentable." And he still keeps his hand over it, slightly embarrassed by the mid-regenerative state of it. "I'm sorry to have disturbed you, I come as unease has taken my mind." And he enters the room and closes the door behind him. "May we sit?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Of course, please, Cerkonos.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You sleep, but uneasily, as all of these realizations swirl in your head. Eventually sleep takes you, but it is not an easy sleep. Just enough to function. Barely. Vax. As you yourself have attempted to stave off sleep for many days, pushing yourself to your limits, it finally takes its toll, and you drift off into a deep, deep slumber. In that slumber, you find yourself in darkness. Echoes of a distant voice, muffled, as if underwater. Crash. The darkness is shattered into a thousand black feathers, and you find yourself falling, falling. Falling towards what seems to be a tangled net of glowing threads. You're afraid you might impact at such a speed, but before you do, you slow and come to a stop before this extremely prominent glowing weave, this infinite tapestry in all directions. You are uncertain of the meaning of this moment when a large cold hand reaches past your shoulder and grasps a thread right before your face, this singular glowing thread, pulsing with light like a heartbeat. You follow the arm over your shoulder, and glance up directly into the massive form of the Raven Queen: porcelain face like a mask, long jet-black hair, her body encasing you like a physical cage. Your eyes follow the arm back to the thread that she carries between her massive fingers. She tugs it, ever so gently, and it causes waves to ripple through the tapestry into infinity. Her voice fills your mind, both calm but cold. "This is you, my champion. You are fate-touched. The choice is yours. Rebirth or ruin. Maker or martyr. Conqueror, tyrant, or nothing." She plucks the thread from the weave, snapping it. You plummet into the hole left behind it, falling faster and faster into the dark. Losing control, you turn around and see the Raven Queen, disappearing into the distance, growing smaller and smaller, until suddenly, nothing. Grog. You fall into a deep snore, heavy, heavy snore, after drinking the wine, climbing up walls, you've had an exhausting day of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Cerkonos. And I'm glad that you're still with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Kaitiaki, Keyleth."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kaitiaki, Cerkonos.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cerkonos leaves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I start poring through my notes for the name Raishan, because Keyleth believes she has heard that name before as one of the dragons, correct? One of the white--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) It's the green one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: --the green one. Okay, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I have any other reference, would I know anything else about that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's all you know, is the name Raishan was used when the Conclave attacked Emon. And it seemed the name belonged to the green dragon that initiated the assault where you guys were.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking bitch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, these thoughts echoing through your mind--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Streets will run green with revenge!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, they will!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The light subsides and your vision returns. You look to see hundreds of people dead at your feet. Farmers, children. You see Pike, motionless, cast among the carnage. You want to scream, but instead you laugh, against your own judgment and intent, like you're locked within yourself and have no control. And as you look up from the pile of corpses at your feet, you see a towering figure before you, cast in furs and bone, standing even a head and a half's height above you, is Kevdak, looking down at you with pride. The entire herd steps up beside him, staring at you atop your throne of corpses, and the herd kneels. Kevdak bows, looking up at you, smiling. "You. You have finally made me proud, Grog." You wake up (gasp), spittle and sand and grit mashed against your face. Who knows how long it's been, but it's been a while since you've had a dream, let alone one like that. Now, as you all eventually come to in the morning, having your evening's rest, you've recovered your hit points, your spells, all of the usual good stuff, in a fantastic mansion. Maybe not the most fantastic rest for some of you, but a fantastic mansion. You all eventually find your way to the breakfast nook, which is as extravagant as you would imagine a Scanlan-based breakfast nook would be.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Phillip! Phillip!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sense others around you, closing in suddenly. A voice, ever-whispering in your ear, says, "Release me." Still blinded, eyes closed, on instinct, you pull the cold, dark blade from your back and swing wide, over and over again, feeling the shuddering shock of each impact, your strength matched by the razor-like edge of your weapon, cleaving down any who would approach. The high of adrenaline is incredible, and you laugh aloud at the sheer thrill of your own power.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs triumphantly)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The light subsides and your vision returns. You look to see hundreds of people dead at your feet. Farmers, children. You see Pike, motionless, cast among the carnage. You want to scream, but instead you laugh, against your own judgment and intent, like you're locked within yourself and have no control.
