Now, as you all eventually come to in the morning, having your evening's rest, you've recovered your hit points, your spells, all of the usual good stuff, in a fantastic mansion. Maybe not the most fantastic rest for some of you, but a fantastic mansion. You all eventually find your way to the breakfast nook, which is as extravagant as you would imagine a Scanlan-based breakfast nook would be.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Phillip! Phillip!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sense others around you, closing in suddenly. A voice, ever-whispering in your ear, says, "Release me." Still blinded, eyes closed, on instinct, you pull the cold, dark blade from your back and swing wide, over and over again, feeling the shuddering shock of each impact, your strength matched by the razor-like edge of your weapon, cleaving down any who would approach. The high of adrenaline is incredible, and you laugh aloud at the sheer thrill of your own power.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs triumphantly)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. So you're going to the Crossroads. The Crossroads is about two days of travel northeast of the Frostweald, about three days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The closest destination. Would the Crossroads be good? Maybe get us to the Crossroads?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or we could just go to Westruun. Is Westruun still there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Westruun's under dragon rule. There's an actual dragon on Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not on. Up in the mountain, but watching down, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But they've got wyverns policing, right, in Westruun?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You didn't notice in your scry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to assume. Let's do the Crossroads.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it! Let's do it! Let's do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We could theoretically run into your herd, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I mean, I hope we do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To the Crossroads!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You complete the spell against one of the petrified trees on the outside of the Cindergrove. The doorway opens up, and you guys can see a thicket of Bramblewood trees on the opposite side. You all rush through one after the other, Trinket jumping behind. You all make it through, and you're now standing on the central pathway of the Bramblewood. Further south, you're probably about an hour and a half foot travel before you exit the southern portion of the forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! I remember that!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We spent a whole day in that park.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. You begin to cast the spell and you concentrate on that tree in the park. There's no connection to the tree. You gather that the tree may not have survived the initial attack on Westruun. However, you do take a moment and you do recall at the entrance to the Bramblewood, when you guys were briefly attacked by that group of thieves that were pulling you off to the side, pretending to be a stranded caravan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There was a series of rather large trees that marked the roadway there, and you recall one that had distinct markings, and you do manage to find a point of concentration or connection there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess it took me a moment to find signal. Okay. All right. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need a signal boost.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think I got one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To the Frostweald? You're about a day and a half. On horseback you're about a day, under a day. On foot, you're looking at about two days' travel, a little over two days' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess it took me a moment to find signal. Okay. All right. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need a signal boost.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think I got one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You complete the spell against one of the petrified trees on the outside of the Cindergrove. The doorway opens up, and you guys can see a thicket of Bramblewood trees on the opposite side. You all rush through one after the other, Trinket jumping behind. You all make it through, and you're now standing on the central pathway of the Bramblewood. Further south, you're probably about an hour and a half foot travel before you exit the southern portion of the forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, sorry, guys, this is as close as I could get us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, this is great!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And how far are we now? A day's travel? Two days' travel, you said?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "About half a day ago. Dusk yesterday. I don't know. We all stay inside around that time just in case."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Then why did they take your daughter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't want to think about it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To keep him working!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look. Say we were really bored, and we wanted to get you your family back. Where would you go?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He points over to the city. "But there's one right out..." And you see him pointing angrily around the corner, probably to the one that you saw on the outskirts of the farm area you guys came down in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to the city then, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's a dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To do what, to attack half-orcs that work for a dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Farmer. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Reginald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Reginald. Reginald, we want to help. How often do you see the dragon flying around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once every couple days, maybe."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Once every couple days. When was the last time you saw him?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I haven't seen anything flying but the dragon. There was a huge fight not more than a week ago between these people and that thing. They seem to have come to an accord."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He points over to the city. "But there's one right out..." And you see him pointing angrily around the corner, probably to the one that you saw on the outskirts of the farm area you guys came down in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to the city then, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's a dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To do what, to attack half-orcs that work for a dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Farmer. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Reginald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Reginald. Reginald, we want to help. How often do you see the dragon flying around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once every couple days, maybe."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Once every couple days. When was the last time you saw him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "About half a day ago. Dusk yesterday. I don't know. We all stay inside around that time just in case."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he have patrols? Does he have wyverns working for him as well, that fly around?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "All of them kept going. We thought we were safe. We tried to re-gather. And then, within a day, a bunch of these bastards came and took what was left. Slaughtering, stealing."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: These people?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The orcs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The orcs, the whatever they are. The big strong angry ones that keep taking all of our stuff."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do they look like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do they look like him, maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Some do."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, fuck me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck, fuck, fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The dragon came in and tore everything down."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn it. Shit hammer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Burned, liquefied, dissolved portions of this city, people screaming. And then it left. Both of them."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll be okay. We're going to keep going. Good luck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, wait, wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, we have to keep going. We can't face these goliaths and dragons and orc guards! This is craziness!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: One of the dragons was green?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "From what we saw."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Green and a black.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Green and a black. I think a white one, but that one kept going."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, we'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And the green one kept going?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: This is an individual that you knew quite a ways ago, a young goliath who was still about a half a foot shorter than you when you knew him. He was named Horace, because he hated it, and Kevdak gave him this name and you actually, at some point in the past, recall even giving Horace a good beating. A little of the smaller of the goliaths you've encountered. This is years later. This punk kid, and he was kind of a punk, has grown up now, and is still smaller than you by a size, but still seems to be every bit of an agitating little bitch you remember, from the way that he's treating these people.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And best we can tell, he's the only guard? The only one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, there's two. There's two.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have an idea!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, he was about to tell us who it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From what you can see, you actually recognize this individual.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fucking nipple tassels!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Okay. We were heading up north. We took out a small caravan just south of the road cuts, near the Silvercut Way, and all of a sudden out of the sky, we saw these streaking dragons come through. Largest I've ever seen. Dangerous as the day's long. So we fled. We watched them come here and tear into this city. Rend it, destroy it, smoke and flames and ice and all sorts of craziness. And they kept going. We figured, dragons come, didn't take the spoils, there's plenty of spoils for us. More than we could ever dream of. Most of it's pretty well-guarded, but there couldn't be much left over there. So we gathered up who we had, and mind you, the herd's swelled a bit since you left, my friend. We conquered another wandering tribe last year. Zanror took the head of their leader and commanded them to join. We're about a hundred strong these days."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, listen. I was going to pounce on this poor farmer over here, but I saw you were obviously keeping your eye on him. Why are you doing that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Which one are you talking about? Reggie?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Old Broken Foot over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "He's a little bitch, that one is. No, but you know what? I caught him sniffing around. I think he's keeping some gold hidden."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Really? Gold? This poor farmer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Twisted his arm a couple of times."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Have you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We could do this for old times' sake. Each take an arm, pull until he tells us."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, like wishbone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that does sound like a good idea. Here's my thing. Why are you guys in this city? It's not really our thing to be in civilized places.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Here's the thing. Let me get loose here for a minute. I'm feeling a bit uncomfortable."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I don't think I will, Horace.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been probably half a day since breakfast. Aye. Why?" His expression begins to shift as realization begins to dawn on his face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, it sounds really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You've missed out, and there's quite a few openings now, since we've lost a few of the less than strong folk. You look strapping. I'm sure Kevdak would be more than happy to have you back."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do. I've actually brought a few gifts.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) I notch an arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I unsheathe Craven Edge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's a good blade."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Isn't it. And I ask out loud, are you hungry?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: His structure is still standing as well. A few buildings around did not fare so well, but his shop and a number of other ones adjacent to it seem to still be standing. You can't really tell if they're functional, or how busy they are, or if anyone's doing anything with them, but that's still there. You do notice, though, at numerous parts of the street, there are just piles and piles of burnt wood and ash, like they're large burn piles that were set at different periods of time and have long since burned. Little bits of white, post-burning plumes that still smolder over the past few days. You're not quite sure what these piles contained at the time they were set ablaze. There is still the worship district here. You have the various temples. Best you can glance, a few of them have been partially toppled. There is damage to some of the buildings. Still too far away to make out any specific details beyond that, some of the structures having taken a hit in previous conflicts. You're not sure from which source.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we see Wilhand anywhere?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this distance, no. You also note, towards the shopping district of Westruun, there is the shop of Abjurist Noja, one of your first enchanter friends who protected you during your first Winter's Crest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! He cast the protection spell on us!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is making me feel very old.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The Margrave's. Towards the southern central portion of Westruun, south of the main town square where the bulk of your first Winter's Crest festival here in Westruun took place. The Margrave's house is at the very southernmost portion of that. That entire square is lined with row after row of sharpened wood sticks and various other weapons, tridents, broken swords that have been, with ropes, strapped together to form a military barricade. If anything were to attempt to attack from the ground, it would be difficult to do so if this area was filled with prepared soldiers, using these as protective guard. The actual Margrave's home is intact, although at this glance, you can see a lot of it has been defiled, elements of the outside walls are strewn with dark burgundy paint slapped over. You can see what looks to be some sort of strings adorning edges of it, like decoration. You see, walking through this area, what looks to be 12 or 13 or so armed individuals of varying heights. Probably a good six or seven goliaths among them. Others appear to be human, half-orc, it's hard to make out the full details from this distance. They're all on the outskirts surrounding what was previously the Margrave's home. Now the homestead of Kevdak, seemingly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about the mayor's house where we heard Horace--?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It is quite a busy time in the afternoon. There is actually a bit of hustle and bustle. Looking around, just a quick glance at your vicinity in Vasselheim, you can see a lot of the bastions are out in force. Many of them are in process of helping construct what looks like reinforced dragon-headed ballistae. Some of the ones you had seen towards the outside walls of the city. There are more being installed upon the inner walls, and the ones in the distance appear to be under some form of construction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know that you all have a lot of errands to do. I don't need everyone to come.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We have a lot of errands to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I thought so. You mentioned...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My only errand is to keep my eyes on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have some errands, if that makes anybody feel better. I have some errands to run.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do you need any help with your errands?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Actually, yes. I would love some help with my errands.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Don't let him shop, Percy. Do not let him shop.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That shouldn't be a problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what that means, but I'm sure I take great offense.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're getting smarter.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How effin' dare you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That eagle form really made a difference.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I spit out a feather. (pthh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. Who's off to where?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where is this Raven Queen's temple?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) We've not been here many times. Are we surrounded by people? Are there people walking about?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "As far as this town is concerned, it takes a lot more than a couple dragons to drop these walls. It's the reason it's been around for a long time, and I'd like to see them try."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, what was the question?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, when you don't take notes when I give you a character's name originally, and they come back around and it's weird when you suddenly take damage and your character starts hemorrhaging.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Loran. Loran was his name.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was just eating an apple. Why am I dying?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Veiled threat.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm just sitting by the fire, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Slayer's Take.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we have seen this guy before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Some of you may have seen him in passing. You haven't really had a conversation with him. He just exists within the guild. There's many members that you've come across in the past.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, that exist.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Probably a dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pointy chin. Got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Stripe on his head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nothing wrong with those.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How are the people doing? Is there a sense of worry, or do you guys feel pretty secure?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The door (whoosh) swings open. You have to duck backwards. It (thump) slams onto the other side of the wall there, knocking a few knick-knacks that were on a nearby shelf over. And Vanessa swings out. "What? Who's calling-- ah. It has been some time. How nice of you to come and visit. May I... be of service to you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we try to sell Vanessa some of our dragon bits?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We should sell to a merchant?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, they tend to be in the--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They may have a buyer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Love that you asked me that question.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, right? What was I thinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Keep a few of the scales, obviously.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can tell what my place in the party is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course. Grog, come with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, wait, keep the teeth. Keep enough teeth for us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll talk about the cold bag for now and see if there's anything else they want to talk about.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have an idea for teeth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I go find Vanessa?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You go and knock on the door. (knocks) There's a brief pause. Knock again. (knocks)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (sing-song) Vanessa? Vox Machina here-- oh, hi.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 23. You understand the Raven Queen has also not always been the one who holds sway over the domain of death, the domain of winter, the domain of fate. Once mortal, the Raven Queen ascended as, essentially, a follower and a partner to the previous god of death, which his name has been erased from the books. This mortal ascended many, many years ago, pre-Second Spark, and has since taken what was once a tyrannical position of death and afterlife and souls and took it instead as the natural transition that it is. However, due to a lack of respect, or at least, a lack of, for lack of a better term, belief or trust from the other gods, she only gained dominion over the actual moment of transition, not of the actual souls of the afterlife. That influence was given to others. But the Raven Queen represents one of the most feared elements of life, which is the end of it, but you also know that that is a natural thing. Your personal belief is up to you. The sense you get here is that... being an individual that worships life, that worships redemption, that worships second chances, you can't help but swallow a little hard at the idea of a god that's purpose is to make sure that if you're destined to have once chance, it stays one chance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of. Or like a very, very dark pair of sunglasses, but made of naturally-formed rock, almost.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Noticing that, I turn and just sort of look at my sister for 5 or 10 more seconds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She doesn't see you and she just stands there quietly, looking around, inspecting the vicinity. Pets Trinket a little.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (hesitantly) Let's do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do I feel anything, being in here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You just feel a natural tremble stepping into a place of powerful divine influence that is not the warm comfort of Sarenrae, which has protected you before. Now, you understand the way many deities work in these lands, and your place in it, and Sarenrae's place in it, and you know enough about the Raven Queen to know that this is not an unwelcome place, per se. Go ahead and roll a religion check.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Then perhaps you'll find those answers one day."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I run over. You okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I stand at the bottom of the stairs and I watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (hoarsely) I got answers in there, but they led to more questions. What am I to do with those questions?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The woman who had led you in there smiles and says, "That is the nature of death. We can't have all the answers, my friend. It is the pursuit of those answers that drives us. Do you trust Her?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I do.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So after about five minutes or so, she grinds down with what materials she has to a little more-- it does mess with some of the polish on the very tip of the metal, but it does come to a more solid point. "Be careful with that."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the rest buckles to the back of the head, so it's a giant metallic groove for the front.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take it. I don't even care if it fits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, that's what I like to hear."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How much is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "This will be 50 gold."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is the point very sharp?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Try it yourself."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And I reach up and I... hit it with my finger.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's all right. It can be sharpened. It's more for decor. It's not meant to slice somebody in half.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it could be sharpened?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I mean, what are you intending to do with this hat? (titters)"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nothing, just look sharp. (groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can do that for you, my big muscley man."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is the very ceiling of intelligence of his.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, see that sweet jam I just dropped there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I did, that was a good line.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Write that in a notebook somewhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll be cleaning that off the floor for ages.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Ooh, all up front? All right. It'll be ready for you shortly." She takes the coin. "Thank you for the patronage. Return here in about... ooh, an hour, and the hat shall be prepared for you, sir-- what's your name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow. I mean, your silhouette is magnificent. Imposing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this your variant fighting costume?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Am I fearsome?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You are. You are. We should go to a silhouette artist after this and get something drawn for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They have those?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They do, they cut it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Milady, you are a master of your trade.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. So they tell me."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Our mom only gave us 60 gold to spend. Could you give us both hats for 60? We're brothers. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Something went wrong with him at birth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check with disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: With disadvantage?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't matter.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, would a 1 be bad? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Deidre goes, "(laughs) You silly, tiny man-- No, seriously. We have a price agreed upon. Pay it."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, I got it. I got 50 gold of my own.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. 20 from me, 50 from him. We'll take them both.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you a few hours and gets you into the early parts of the evening, but you do manage to go ahead and complete it. You manage to get it finished up and you're pretty proud of your work, actually, and you feel-- the whistle you're still doing as you do the final bits, you realize is the song that Scanlan sang for you, just looping in your head. It happened to push you through your work and get your final piece completed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (hums Black Hole Sun)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23. What did you roll naturally?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Plus 10.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Plus 10.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Made it 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wow. All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's not complicated, but I've got a good design, and it has two nifty things.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What are the nifty things?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fingers.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't actually have fingers. It's a mitt piece, strap-on, it can lock the thumb and lock the hand so you can open it, lock, close it, lock. It's got little flip-locks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can detach the hand and put a pick spike on for mining. Like a mining punch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is it like Young Frankenstein? (mechanical sound effects)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's more Dustin Hoffman "Hook", but, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was thinking Go-Go-Gadget Finger.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Because it's got the click, pull, click.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can do it with all these things. I can graph it. So.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Reasonable. It's not pretty.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. As the sun begins to set in Vasselheim, you make your way back over to the Braving Grounds, which as you look across, too, as the night goes dark, the Braving Grounds itself has a whole different life to it. There are so many forges and smelters going that there's-- most buildings have this bright orange glow through the ceiling and these various openings where fumes and smoke and exhaust are sent up from the various production constructs and such. It has this very luminescent look in comparison to a lot of the city. It's very interesting. It reminds you of the deep, deep portions of Kraghammer, where most of the actual fire and melted rock was being smelted and gathered and heated. So you make your way to the hovel. The roof workers are no longer there, and the door is closed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Reasonable. It's not pretty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you a few hours and gets you into the early parts of the evening, but you do manage to go ahead and complete it. You manage to get it finished up and you're pretty proud of your work, actually, and you feel-- the whistle you're still doing as you do the final bits, you realize is the song that Scanlan sang for you, just looping in your head. It happened to push you through your work and get your final piece completed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I also carved a little message inside that will not matter unless it actually becomes pertinent.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You don't get to know until you ask offscreen.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Ohhh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm excited. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's right, DM.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That never happens.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Try me. Okay. Do you go and deliver it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I do go and deliver it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It has been steeped way too long.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to stop and attach and begin strapping this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "(confused stuttering)"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let me show you. It attaches like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ooh."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Here are the two switches. (clicking) And for your mine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Eh? Oh."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I grab, hit the button, twist, pull.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh? Eh? I just got that!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And I get the spike and attach it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Remove it, put the hand back on. Tea?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chuckle, click) (excited laughter) "Tea?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Why not? Always say yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Need another cup. (grunts)" He stands up and goes over, still holding the kettle and splashing everywhere, and he's flipping through cabinets and knocking pieces of glass out. "Ah! Found it." Pulls out another little ceramic cup and pours the tea in, sets it down. (chink, click) "(cackles)"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To new hands!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To new hands.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a great toast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is pungent. It is bitter.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, as you guys walk outside of the Slayer's Take guildhall, you find the nearest tree, which you've used before to traverse. As you all step through the portal, Percy and Scanlan look over their shoulder at the distant skyport and see the one airship docked there and think, "One day..."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have an instant ability to transport anywhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It would be so cool, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Then we wouldn't have to go to Whitestone. It would hold all of our things.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: When we kill all the dragons...

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We could hold all of our things on the airship, and I could have cannonades...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That will be the first thing we do when Thordak dies is we jump on an airship.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Party on the airship.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll buy it with all of the gold that we've stolen from all the dragons. It'll be great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We won't need the airship if the dragons are dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All the fun has gone out of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (piercing spell noise)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Before we leave, do you want to sell these stupid white dragon scales?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (repeats piercing spell noise) Through a tree!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys emerge. Due to the time differences, you land here in Whitestone, looking at where the sun is in the sky, it's early afternoon, as opposed to the morning where you guys just stepped away from. There are people in the streets, and you see a few, a little bit of life has returned to this town square, though still, by comparison-- there are folks now that are bringing in actual, healthy, looks to be fruits from nearby trees. There's a little bit of trade going on. This is the first real chance you've had to see a little bit of Whitestone coming to life again. As you emerge from the tree, a few individuals nearby stop and look and smile and wave, recognizing you as you approach.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Like kill us all? No no no. Say they go first. "Go, go, go, go." And I kind of start teleporting through the tree and while I'm in the Sun Tree I just want to be like, "Hey, boo."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: "I missed you. Have you heard of this whole cancerous tree situation in the Feywild?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) You're talking to a tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shh, no. I'm in the middle of the tree. I'm in the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're stuck in the tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you finish this thought, you hear this sound emerge (vacuum sealing sound) and realize it's the sound of the whole portal you've made closing behind you because it only lasts for six seconds and you should probably get the hell out of the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aww. I step out of the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Good try.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you step out, a voice goes, "Hey, boo, sorry.(kiss noise)" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And it closes behind you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll talk to him later.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "So I've heard from all sorts of interesting folk. I only hope one day to wear clothes as fine as you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You... Gilmore. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Walks over to you, Pike, and kneels down and gives you a little pinch on the cheek and is like, "Looking as spry and smiley as ever, I see."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hello, Shaun. (more laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I give him a kiss on the cheek.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ooh."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "Ooh."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He turns to you. "Vex'ahlia, look at you. I hear, if my words say correct, that you folks are a recent dragon-slaying bunch, hmm?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That we are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "(chuckles) Good to know."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Skips over you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Save the best for last.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And goes to Scanlan and goes to ruffle your hair, and goes, "And you, you, my little bardish friend."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Little?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, you're actually physically little."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You are a tinier person."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Embrace it! It's what makes you different."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's true, I suppose. I suppose. Yes, absolutely. We've been spreading the good word about you and your works.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. Not a worry. All right, so as the sun sets, you find your way down to the proper dinner table. Gilmore is there, Eskil is there, Cassandra is there...

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I just. I don't know. It's still something that I need to think about.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm just.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You pray to your Sarenrae about it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (laughs) You do look lovely, though, in your dress.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You look beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You look so cute.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I do a twirl. (more laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do a little air cantrip to make the skirt poof out.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Ooh.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I tug up my dress.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, do you guys mind tying me up a bit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nice and tight. (grunts)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you breathe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (strained) No, but that means it's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I assume there's clean clothes for everybody in their quarters?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's all good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm wearing the same shit. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And the big hat?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) To dinner. (shoonk)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) Yeah. I paid money for this thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to arrange for our stuff to be stowed in the vault.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Within the next 15 minutes or so, before food is brought to the table, Allura finally enters in a very, very nice dress, and says, "It is so good to see you all are safe."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And the big hat?

preceding_turns.text: You guys look fantastic.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. We do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I am dressed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm dressed, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'll dress up. I'll dress up for dinner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. As you guys sit down, pleasantries are exchanged-- (yawns) Pardon me. You catch up a bit with Cassandra. Partway through--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Aw, Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: E3, y'all.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sit down, have a beer, watch our game.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) 2 pm.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well..." She looks over this way and Kima walks in, for the first time you've seen her really out of armor since you first saw her. Her hair is also in similar braids to her side and she herself is wearing a golden dress, though she's kind of like...

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We look fabulous.

preceding_turns.text: You guys look fantastic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Allura!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hello.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I give her a hug.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She then gives you a big, wonderful hug.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How's Kima? We haven't seen her yet.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We're still delving into it, and it may be a little while before we figure this out, but this is the best I can ascertain with my knowledge at my disposal. And the Realmseer and Gilmore can back up this research. We've all worked together these past weeks and we're all kind of coming to the same conclusion." She gestures to Gilmore. Gilmore says, "I've only been on this case personally for a few days of rising health standards in my body and I've thrown everything I can at it, honestly, and it's all just come to nothing. Gone. (pthbbt)"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So it's fair to assume that at the other end of this is something old, something trapped, something hungry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Would it be in this plane or another?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have no idea. Everything we've attempted to put through it that had any sort of magical connection, whether it be through inter-planal scrying, anything that would bring us to the other side of this, is crushed, consumed, and transferred as part of it. None of our rituals or enchantments will let us see through this spinning marble, as you call it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You haven't put any blood through it, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's not.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm hoping that hasn't happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Seemingly, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Or is it? Don't know? Is it that you have no idea what happened to things put through?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Hundred miles. There were reports of the white dragon a week ago, south of us. Far south of us."

preceding_turns.text: Maybe we can get one of them on our side.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can only be so hopeful that they tear each other apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe the white one will want to help kill Thordak with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Turning them against each other is not a bad idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Our best hope, at this point, is just to amass power. We are not strong enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But we will get stronger.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does the heart-stone make him even stronger than he already was?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know, but he was not that large when we sealed him."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Seize.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we can take-- yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: If we can pry it out of his body, we could shrink him down. Perfect plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Then we could go inside a dragon again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can go in another dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Every one of them, one after the other.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You seen any dragons on the outskirts of the town? Have there been any flyovers or close calls within, say, 50 to 100 miles or so? Any reports?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We're just getting now to that point. It may be a few weeks before we're ready to go. It's a lot to build and it's a very big space to protect, but me and Drake are working on some ideas. (sighs)"

preceding_turns.text: What about means of protection for Whitestone. Do you have a defense planned? A warning system?

preceding_turns.text: Wow.

speaker: MATT

You eventually open an intricate door and walk out onto a wide balcony on that tower that overlooks the grounds and the castle before you. Beautiful moonlight, the night air. You see the moon is nearly full and the white light illuminates the densely-forested countryside surrounding the castle. He closes the door and walks up to you at the edge of the balcony, looking over it all, elbows on the edge. "Some time has passed, Vax. And much of the world moves on to newer, greater threats. But true pain never really heals." He turns to you and steps up to you, looking you close in the face. "You hurt me deeply. And continue to hurt. Agony, for weeks, months. speaker: MATT
raw_text: 22 points of necrotic damage. So as you leap into the sphere, you feel as if the gravity that holds you to the ground, or sometimes the ceiling, bends around you and your limbs are wrenched forward, like someone has you in a full Nelson that doesn't end. Your body is about to be condensed and crushed, when you feel the symbol pulse, and the gravity lessens somewhat. It's still tearing into you, and you feel this coldness and this numbness come over your body, as you feel like you're a square peg being pushed through a round hole, physically pushed through against your will. You begin to yell out in pain instinctually before it comes to a stop and you all land to your feet, out of breath. The numbness begins to fade to immediate pins and needles, just pain across your entire body. You feel like someone just raked a number of tacks across every surface of your skin and as the pain begins to throb, you can feel the blood pulsing throughout your body as it all shoots to the surface. You still sense that you, thanks to your symbols around your neck, you managed to withstand a much more dangerous arrival. As your eyes focus, you already feel the chilled air that surrounds you, like a cold, cold winter morning. The sky above is sunless and gray, like an overcast horizon without clouds to texture it, breached by distant mountain ranges like so much broken glass. You see behind you immediately the other corresponding siphon, floating, bright gray-white in color and glowing, the same color that you saw briefly shifted to in the ziggurat near Ank'harel. Beneath it, you find shards of ruined objects, piles of long-dead bodies, flesh and bone rended and twisted like they'd been put through some rapid tornado on different sides of their body. And you see a lot of these shattered pieces of wood and metal were all a number of objects that were thrown in here during the experimental phase when you guys first uncovered this. You look around and notice that you stand atop a rectangular platform of dark, smooth stone, about 15 feet on each side. Beneath the edges, about 20 feet down, 'til it comes to the ground. So you're on an elevated stone platform that the siphon is affixed to above, floating about four feet above the top of the surface. And you look down to the ground immediately around you and you see just dry, cracked badlands. It seems to spread out, making up the majority of the Shadowfell around you. Surrounding your location, your sight clarifies to see what appears to be hundreds of buildings and structures that form this shade of an ancient city, like a town, a sprawling village. Half the structures toppled and destroyed, piles of rubble and rock. The ground itself seems to jut up in places, these tiny, shooting spires of black and gray earth that the city has either been built around or seemed to have grown through. As you look past that, you see movement throughout the streets in the distance, but before you can focus on that, your vision immediately sticks towards, in the center of this circular-like city, the standing, ominous, commanding, massive spire of obsidian black, familiar to both of you through two different types of vision you've experienced. A jagged monolith that exudes ancient hatred, encircled by dozens of dark, winged beasts that watch over the dreary skies. Who here is proficient in history?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Enon.

preceding_turns.text: That's correct.

preceding_turns.text: "No, what's going on?"

preceding_turns.text: (louder) Enon turned into a zombie! (whispering) And was killed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry to hear that. That's terrible. Wow, that's really terrible. I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.text: We were trying to see if he might have worked with people that might have known what he was doing that day, earlier.

raw_text: "Yeah. We don't get many shows and traveling folk. Last time this was here, a year ago, it came and went in a week."

preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry to hear that. That's terrible. Wow, that's really terrible. I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We were trying to see if he might have worked with people that might have known what he was doing that day, earlier.

preceding_turns.text: "Because there's a circus in town."

You recognize a lot of them as people that were in the lounge earlier before you entered this actual arena. They're carrying a small chest of iron and dark set wood and as they place it on the ground, they begin to pull out sacks of money. They're presenting you the divided prize. That's three rounds for the group and each round was 200 gold. That's 600 gold that you guys get to divide and then the final pot was 2000 divided amongst the two teams. An additional 1000 to your team. So 1600 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You overhear the gist of most of the conversation, best you can without catching too much attention to yourself. Over the next 20 minutes or so, you catch what amounts to them discussing possible future difficulty in crossing border boundaries between the empire, the Menagerie Coast, and imports. The necessity of them, but having to make sure they go through very stringent processes in doing so, with the promise of coming conflict. He doesn't outright say that they're at war yet, but the Starosta is essentially laying the groundwork for maintaining trade lines through the Wuyun Gorge, across the Cyrios Mountains. Wanting to make sure that the port towns of the Menagerie Coast still have access to get the necessary goods to the empire, not only to maintain their people, but to get them as quickly as possible to the front to the east.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but you also dropped a horse apple in front of them as well.

preceding_turns.text: It didn't devolve into a punching fest. That's pretty good.

preceding_turns.text: Is the Starosta still here?

All the luck in your future endeavors."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Finish the evening off right. I'll honestly say I'd like to get out of here as soon as possible so they don't think we're running from the news. We get out of here before the news hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough. All at once?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where do we go?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't care. I'm just happier moving than staying still.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How quickly are you thinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If it were just the two of us, I'd say leave tonight, but--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How about 24 hours from now?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: See if we can make it faster, but 24 hours is not terrifying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Dusk tomorrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No later than dusk. I would like to get out before news starts to spread. Before they decide to start locking down the holes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. One more day. Just to get supplies and get ready for the road again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a fair point.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we divide up the money?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I believe that's 228 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Northeast-ish. East of Rexxentrum. That's where we've been stationed for the past six or seven months or so. Done a few jobs here and there."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we divide up the money?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I believe that's 228 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Same to you. It was very impressive. Where does fate take you after this night?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, as far as I know, we're probably heading back up to Nogvurot and continuing with our contract of sitting around and being paid for nothing."

speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. Legal trouble, or illegal trouble?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What you will.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you're going to go illegal, you can actually go just beyond the boundaries of the Empire to Shadycreek Run. Entire haven for murderers, thieves, and all sorts of strange people."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe if it's not so gnarly?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is Deastok to the west, as well as Kamordah. There's quite good drink in Kamordah. There's Pride's Call if you like the jovial dwarvenfolk there. preceding_turns.text: We hear that. Soltryce Academy is located there as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, right. Yes-- "

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My friends call me Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Fair enough. What can I do for you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I couldn't help but notice your body posture, for this whole party, has been rather tense. I don't know if you noticed, but I am a bit of a healer, and I'm from the Menagerie Coast, where I did a lot of mental healing, as well as body healing. If there's anything you need to relieve your stress, I'm very good at this. This is why I came here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He gives a look and goes, "Uh," and looks back and forth between his companions. He goes, "What are you insinuating?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm talking about somebody to talk to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right! Right."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To relieve your mind of your stress!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right. I really appreciate that offer. Genuinely, Jester Fancypants McGee. However, such is my life-- "

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know you have people that work for you--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Since you've been in town? It's probably been a week and a half to two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I am a healer! I'm a really good healer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you tell him that he has to give you a dollar to engage your services?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, I told him-- I didn't give him a price, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, that's even shiftier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That means I'm really good, is what that means.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You charge for these services?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not for friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) I'm actually really shit at giving advice, but don't tell anyone, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't agree. I feel like you would be very good at it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, any time you have any problems, Yasha, you just come to me, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm maybe showing a little bit of pink on my cheeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we talking about rolling at the Pillow Trove this evening? Is that what I am picking up on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I need to go by there anyway. How long has it been since we've been in town?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Jester. Hold on just a second." She gets up and walks out of the room. A minute passes. Two minutes pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's it?! Four gold! Do you know how much she wanted to charge me before? I'm going to leave it though, because I am walking away. I'm at the front desk.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Four gold for two rooms?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For each room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll cover the fellas' room. Here's four gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Here's four more for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This was her ceding to the Victory Pit victory and return customer price. Jester, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to see if my package is here from my mom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you head to where you went previously, which is a little side chamber. There's a young woman there waiting, who is falling asleep at the desk. "Oh, I'm sorry! I didn't see you there. Can I help you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I blend in with my environment very often. It's common.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. Well, very well done."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have a package here, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Jester.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About five minutes pass before she enters with a small parcel under her arm, and goes, "I believe this is to you, Jester, correct?" It's a small box about that big, it says "Jester" written in nice handwriting on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.text: Anything on the desk of value?

preceding_turns.text: On the desk of this woman who has left? There looks to be a small oil lantern set there that's flickering. It's well-made.

preceding_turns.text: Nice pen or anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it looks like she took it with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I keep waiting.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No. First things first. The skies are gray. What little bits of sunlight came through the last day have completely absconded. The sky is covered in gray, nearly stormy clouds. There is an air of stress and question in the streets as you start seeing Righteous Brand groups making their way through. As you make your way through the north side of the Pentamarket, one of the town criers comes by shouting, "War declared on the Kryn by the King in retribution of attack on eastern garrisons!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Where are y'all going?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We have to take a trip to Pumat's for supplies and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Insight check! Just kidding. I'm good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Who's going where?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go wherever Caleb goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pumat, library.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pumat, library. The rest of you guys are going to Dolan's?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To Dolan's. We'll meet up at Pumat's.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That sounds good. You guys, Dolan's; we'll go library.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll do library first. Then we'll pull Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll pull Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can pull Matt's soul.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pull Matt Key.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. As you guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (silly voice) Hey, you got your chocolate in my peanut butter!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys exit the Pillow Trove, and you make your way outside of the Tri-Spires, the first thing of note is the tone and the mood of the city has shifted dramatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a more festive atmosphere?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a party, girl.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Another one, a few moments later further down the way shouts out, "Righteous Brand mobilizing east to protect our families! They're grabbing horses from nearby stables, and it seems like since the close of the festival, through the night and into the morning, preparations have already kicked in on the military side.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I see you are aware of the shifts in our current imperial state of affairs. We haven't had war in generations. I'm hopeful that the conflict is swift, but I'll leave that concern to those with the right expertise. I'm tasked with keeping my people safe here, not on the front. Here. Now, wartime puts us in an unfortunate scenario. Zadash effectively governs the southern half of the empire and most of military might is now leaving us. Our Crownsguard will be stretched thin across keeping the peace and dealing with unrest and worry and protecting these walls from whatever pushes through the lines of battle and the general things that wander from beyond our boundaries. Money isn't an issue, but personnel is. There are outlying threats and criminals we no longer have the manpower for and as such, the Crown has approved an expanded mercenary budget. Now you've already proven your ability to me twice. Most recent, your skills in the Victory Pit showing you capable and loyal to the king. I know not your interest, but if you would come under contact, under my office as legionnaires of the empire, you would be responsible for protecting the lives of many innocent people. Now, under contract you would receive 15 gold a week per person as a stipend whether or not you were on contract or not. It could be at your leisure, months at a time with no work and you still get paid no matter what you do. Each assignment rewards its own bounty of course, case by case. The work I'll be presenting would largely involve the hunting of outstanding criminals, the acquisition of resources that may benefit the Empire in this time of war, and sometimes the extermination of dangerous creatures that threaten life away from the front lines. Now you'd all have credentials within the Empire and that will grant you access to supplies, pro bono. Access to empirical halls, and individuals otherwise restricted to the general populace."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, we were right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's fantastic. Please, have a seat. I'd like to discuss a few things with you if you don't mind. First off, congratulations yesterday. That was quite a display and a fantastic victory. Well--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we going to war?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few tomes are retrieved. "This here is a recollection of what elements of historical significance could be pulled from the remnants of the Age of Arcanum, pre-Calamity. There are a great number of mages out there, though many of them befell darker interests. But here are those books. If you're looking for more modern mages, we have historical information on the past 200 years of empirical magecraft and powerful wizards in that realm."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Memberships.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are led to a section. Madeer reads through. "Arcanists, you say? Historically? Are we talking Age of Arcanum?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am looking for histories, accounts of people who pushed the boundaries of what can be done over the ages. The firsts. I am interested in more than what is typical, at least as much as can be known. Do you follow me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I believe so."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's a magic fan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, that is true, I am a magic fan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Little heavy-handed, man. Like, really heavy-handed.

raw_text: You're a speed reader, but this is also very dense material, and it is essentially a historical theorizing on historical elements they've been able to piece together. I'd say by the time you've gotten here, it'd be about noon in the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That would be more Soltryce Academy material. They do lay claim to most of those theoretical discussions and research. The Cerberus Assembly would be the keepers of those research endeavors."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Excellent, I shall go there next.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have a book here on the Cerberus Assembly, if that would be of interest to you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These tomes are presented to you. You can spend time to read if you'd like. Madeer watches the entire time, like a hawk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start reading.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Are you absorbing it, or are you skimming through?

preceding_turns.text: This one, I am absorbing it. I will read it cover to cover.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cover to cover will take you the better part of the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You read through that tome. It's about three in the afternoon now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.text: Cool, cool.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It has a breakdown of previous members who passed away. It's basically a historical dialogue of how it was established. The name was actually a reference to a group that existed in the Age of Arcanum. It talks about how there was a war of mage houses 150 years ago within the empire that nearly destroyed part of the capital, Rexxentrum, and as part of the ceasefire and the arrest of these mage houses that were at war, as opposed to imprisoning them, they agreed to bind themselves to the empire and work for them. That's what the Cerberus Assembly was established as: a power under rule of the king. Since then has flourished. It's helped the kingdom rise to power, it helped them conquer the Julous Dominion. They work with and under the king. It talks of all the members and it talks about what they do. Trent is in there. There is Master Trent Ikithon, who is the Archmage of Civil Influence. Civil Influence is the title and it talks about how he is the propagandist and the constructor of the face of the Empire in upper-echelon circles. It speaks of Martinet Lunidus Da'leth who is the Archmage of Domestic Protections, who is the oldest in the original member of the Assembly who still lives. There is Master Doolan Tversky who is the Archmage of Dysology.

preceding_turns.text: "Let me go ahead and check there for a second, those are generally smaller-scale here. Pumat III, if you don't mind." "I got it, already listening to you, don't worry."
