preceding_turns.text: I think we make a good team. I think there's more to be had here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It might run out, who knows. I'm not an optimist, but think there's still more to be had.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do we make a polite exit at the least or do we just bugger out?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She seemed pretty heartfelt, I think a polite exit would be thoughtful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm fair for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Merchants' guild then polite exit?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, maybe we'll rustle up W.C., Loo, John, Crapper, Shitter, and Toilet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh that's right, our horsies!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Any other business you want to do before you decide to leave the city?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you want to swing by and see if-- Yeah, can we pop into the other shop? The old Grace of the Wild and see if they have any healing potions, just to check out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just in case.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In the interest of time...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do they have any?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You gather the knowledge of this war has blindsided most of the merchant guilds in Zadash. Best you can tell, most of them are in the process of getting ready to figure out what their next endeavor is. Those that would be leaving won't be leaving for a few days. If you hang around, you may be able to pick up that kind of work.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ointments.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I ask a question?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: With a healer's kit, can you heal people when you're not in combat? Or is it just for stabilizing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The healer's kit usually can be used to stabilize somebody. There's a feat you can take that allows you to spend an action to heal someone with it in combat. It's called the Healer feat. Which is actually pretty cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. I'll look at that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It turns anybody into a combat healer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go say goodbye to somebody and leave?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we checking the merchants' to see if there's any armed escort requests out of the merchants' guild?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Who's helming that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fjord!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm coming with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll help you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Us three. Oh. That was bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's helping you with it. It's advantage on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes! (laughs) Oh no! Slightly better. Five.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No. He actually was a legionnaire for us about five years back, but then went rogue, and we believe had ties with the Myriad and became a powerful tent-pole here in the Marrow Valley. We had him cornered in Deastok about three months ago by a team of crownsguard under the leadership of Carlos Ventuga. But this Hawker slew Carlos and fled south into the Wuyun Gorge and disappeared into the Menagerie Coast. We're having to send whoever we can to try and retrieve him and bring him back alive."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think we are probably not going to be able to help out right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I understand."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's not you. It's us. We're not ready for that kind of commitment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I understand, that's all right."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're going to make an adventuring party very happy some day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You really will.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I hope so. We'll send someone else after the Hawker."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: After the what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Hawker."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's a Hawker?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Don't worry about it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Does anyone know what a Hawker is?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Which spelling are you using for that word? A-W-K? O-C-K?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: H-A-W-K.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like a monster or something?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We sent a troupe after him about eight weeks ago, and I fear they may not be coming back at this point. We intended to send some of our top investigators from the capital after this bounty--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: From the Menagerie Coast?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The opposite direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The Menagerie Coast is really freaking cool, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So many choices!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, that sounds really cool!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you need more time, that's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you guys think? Hold on, can we talk about this?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Quick huddle.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pros and cons.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm happy just to leave the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pros, we get to visit the Menagerie Coast, which is always really cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pro, 10,000 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do we have a time limit on when we'd be able to get him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, but the longer it takes, the easier he probably has vanishing into the south."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Makes sense.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's late afternoon at this point. You guys are coming back right as you met with Kara there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit. I'm unstoppable!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's part of the reason that Vax survived so much in the last campaign.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think I get Revivify now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I use that once a day? Once a round?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's just your reaction, once a round.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Once a round?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit. I'm a golden god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I get a new blood curse. I want to have a conversation about that before we go into that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you do get another blood curse, you get to choose.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have Dispel Magic you guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So does Yasha, no big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, that's a big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you get, of course, 3rd-level spells, and you get another invocation. You can mess with it later.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where we're leaving off, are we going to come back? Because it's the end of the day, are we going to come back at the end of the day or are we going to come back next morning?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You gathered your things and went back to the open road where you headed to the crossroads, did some trading. Through the evening, jumped into a few old friends who were looking to rob you, sent them on their way. Then continued northward until eventually you came upon the outskirts of the-- what was it-- Silberquel Ridge-- there we go, brain not working-- to the gnomish city of Hupperdook. Upon arriving in the city, you took in the industrial sites: the elements of it that are heavy machinery and the creation of new types of clockwork-based technologies, the stacks of soot and steam sent outward, the war machines being developed for use to the eastern lines where the conflict with Xhorhas is now brewing. You made a friend by the name of Rissa who said, in exchange for a round drinks in the evening, would show you around the town for a bit. Upon making your way to the upper level, you were given a brief tour of the vicinity which seemed strangely empty. The streets very well decorated for some seeming celebration, but nobody present. As the sun slowly went down, the town came to life as all the work whistles blew, the sounds of merriment began. And suddenly, as the sun goes vanishing behind the distant mountains, the golds and oranges and subtle purples of the sunset begin to take over, the sound of music and laughter begins to take the streets. So, Mighty Nein, what would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Short and sweet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then, without further ado  let's jump in to tonight's episode of Critical Role. [click, TV static] [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello everyone, and welcome back. That's good. Last we left off: The Mighty Nein had completed their first of two ventures they agreed to complete in the name of The Gentleman. Heading northward towards Berleben, traversing the dangerous Labenda Swamp, freeing the once endangered safe house from the encroaching merrow dangers that had destroyed and decimated the people that were working there at the time and freeing it up for future use. You had acquired a bird friend in the tiny Kiri that is along with you, dagger in hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Six hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's fine. You'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's going to die.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You gathered your things and went back to the open road where you headed to the crossroads, did some trading. preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Short and sweet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then, without further ado  let's jump in to tonight's episode of Critical Role. [click, TV static] [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Six hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's fine. You'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's going to die.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So the better part of 15 minutes pass of you guys waiting there and being overlooked until eventually the dwarf woman stops and goes, "All right, sorry about that, didn't see you there. You're a bit higher than the usual folk. What can I do for you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you say she was a dwarf?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A dwarf, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who do we talk to? Do we talk to the gnome or the dwarf?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Whichever one we catch the eye of first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we should do the bar lean in thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shove some other people around, do that thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For the hell of it, both of you guys roll charisma checks, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this is going to be funny.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Something we're great at. Just straight checks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just roll a d20 and add your charisma modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That dice just is the biggest tease.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Want to go at the same time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It rocks back and forth. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Three, two, one. Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Hold that till later on. That event's not till later tonight."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you see?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I saw." Cracks his knuckles, sets his music down in front, starts getting ready. The other performers to the side are watching this out of the corner of their eye and laughing. They're enjoying the tension that's brewing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I run over and give Caleb a kiss on the cheek. Thank you, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys get your rooms situated. As the keys are being passed out to the different rooms, drinks are being handled. "So are you looking for something strong or something strong?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Amaze me, baffle me, make me forget where I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She leans into you and goes "I like you. You're colorful."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bless you. You're colorful yourself. I love everything you're wearing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you. First one's on me, how about that?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bless you. I'll happily pay for hers as well because I want to drink her under the fucking table.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You and Rissa go into a full run. You beating her easily. You get to the table and scoot into the side. As she approaches, you watch as two male gnomes intercept her, and block her path. One of them goes, "Uh-uh, sorry Tinkertop, table's not for you." She goes, "Fuck off, Fitz! I've worked hard enough. I saw this table fair and square." The other one goes, "You don't work, Tabletop, just like your brain-sick dad. This table's for working folk." Fitz glances over and says, "Go ask your pops to make you a table to drink at alone. Might buy you an hour before it breaks."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, I've never seen anyone actually swing from a chandelier. I just thought it was a metaphor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, that's entirely something you can do. It's more common than you'd think.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it allowed in other bars?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you talking to Rissa? "I imagine if they're not paying attention."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There was some pub. I don't remember what town I was in, but I do remember the pub because it was actually called the Swinging Chandelier. I don't remember what town it was, though. But it was a great pub. It had the best chandelier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "My gnomish eyes spot what looks to be one open table across the way, right there." Rissa points, and you can see, past the way, there's one table that two people are just getting up from and leaving.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will dart at full speed, full dash to get that table.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She curls into the bench around the table and leans one shoulder onto it. "My father's an inventor, a tinkerer of some renown at one time. He's had a rough few years."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Some people can't hold their liquor. It's really embarrassing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's sad, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They both just get lost into the crowd.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, stay, play with us longer!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Rissa looks over her shoulder at them leave and (laughing).

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wow, that took a turn fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I like the way that worked out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Me too, indeed!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who were they?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Fitz and Ashton. They're both a couple of punks. I've known them since I was a kid."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They called you "Tabletop."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, Tinkertop."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tinkertop.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Both equally mysterious.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait. So your dad is, what, rich or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's nothing to be ashamed of. It's okay if he is.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "From what I've seen, it can depend on if people are that interested or that competitive. Some folks put up the few gold they've earned for a few days if they're feeling lucky. I've seen people put upwards of 20, 25 gold sometimes, which is quite a sizable sum."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A team drinking competition?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The remainder gets the coin, I think a percentage goes to the house and gets to take the title and a token. That token is worth a free drink to each member of that team in this bar every night."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Every night?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is almost like a drunken CrossFit gym. I'm sorry. It is almost like a CrossFit gym.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should probably do that. For this, I would make an exception.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It could cost a lot of money.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, he said whoever contributes the most monetarily gets to take a shot at the honor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much do people usually contribute?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have two gold.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You've probably been here for the better part of an hour or two, just getting your paces, having drinks and taking in the performances and the music. "I'd say you probably have another hour before it begins."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It could cost a lot of money.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, he said whoever contributes the most monetarily gets to take a shot at the honor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much do people usually contribute?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have two gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "From what I've seen, it can depend on if people are that interested or that competitive. Some folks put up the few gold they've earned for a few days if they're feeling lucky. I've seen people put upwards of 20, 25 gold sometimes, which is quite a sizable sum."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you a big drinker, Rissa?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can hold my weight, but I'm not much of a competitor, if that's what you're asking."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it happening relatively soon, or do we have a few more hours to explore a bit? I just have the feeling if we were to engage, that all activities after that would not be documented in the ol' brain so well.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, it's been a while since I've--"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She is a wallflower tonight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She does seem very uncomfortable, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I still want to check the price, so I go up to the bar.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you approach the bar, the dwarven woman, who's in the process of cleaning a spill on the side, looks over and sees you and does a triple-take, like-- "Well, would you look at this strapping mass of green and tooth." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you kindly, ma'am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What can I do for you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's a little early in the evening, but I thought I might get a head start and inquire what some friendship might run me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Mighty forward, but I can't say I'm not interested. I've got a few more hours on my shift, but if you hang around--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I would hate to remove you from your duties. Plus, my companions are also interested in the same. You said there might be--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "For an hour? Three? That'd put you about, say, 20 gold."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I would hate to remove you from your duties. Plus, my companions are also interested in the same. You said there might be--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, it's been a while since I've--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh! No, yeah, let me clarify. (stammering) I need--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She goes and starts pouring herself a drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Multiples-- not multiples! I need individual companions for my friends, not just your beautiful self.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How many?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Three.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Three companions? All right. I have to go and connect with a friend here and see what's available, but that's certainly doable."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Great, and do you know how much that might run?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, that depends on what you're asking for. Is it only for the hour, is it for the evening, what the requests are--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Per hour is good, yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: They're all pretty much gnomish middle-age, which could be anywhere between 100 to 200 years old, and you can see the weathering on their faces and their clothing. They look like they nearly drink professionally. They step up to the table and they figure out what order they're going to go in. You guys figure out what order you're going to go in?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The Mighty Nein.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Mighty Nein!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If I want to be known for something, this is exactly what I want to be known for.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cheers and clamoring continue around, and everyone starts shouting and yelling. "All right, let's set up these drinks people!" With that final slam on the table, she leaps off onto the ground and starts heading off towards the bar on the side. The other bartender-- the gnomish gentleman there is rapidly clearing out tankards and drinks, and he's starting to pull up bottles onto the countertop. They start pushing tables into the center again once more as the crowds begin to circle around, and Ol' Blemmy and the rest of his rather rough-looking crew that have seen some life in their time--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fraternity all-stars.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you both finish the drink, everyone is like, "Yeah! Oh--" The whole crowd backs up for a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I got too cocky!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You manage to catch most of it and you finish it. You realize that the liquors that you've been having are not been mixing well with this particular drink. You're starting to feel-- oof. That's a victory on his end.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All you hear is this subtle (chuckling). Roll for the next one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't think I can do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Jester cut him! He can't see you out there, cut him!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cut my eyes! Cut my eyelids off!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What? No! You got this, Nott! I'm going to cast Bless.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what does that do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think it gives you advantage on saving throws. Is this a saving throw?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, but you're blessed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I cast it on you!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, make your roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He rolled a natural 16 with a plus four modifier, that puts him at 20. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (chanting) Break the tank! Break the tank!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We will in the morning."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well worth it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is there money involved here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was double or nothing, so that's 60 gold. They end up having to pony up the rest of it. There's a 20% cut for the house though, so that's 60 gold, losing 20% of that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take 20 of that because I put in--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's 48 gold go back to you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I take five because I gave Molly five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Everyone claps you on the back and starts cheering and shaking your hands and stuff. "Oh, that was amazing!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tell me you have biscuits and gravy or something.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Caleb, a moment passes before Jester begins leading you, but you guys continue, the two of you, to have a very nice little waltz in the middle of the area. Quietly, the music begins to change. What was once the rowdy bar music transitions into an actual waltz piece as the musicians notice this happening. The three fourths begins to pick up. One two three, one two three. As you guys continue to dance, a few other couples begin to get up and start faking it. They don't seem to know, but they're watching, playfully making up their own waltz-type maneuver. An impromptu, half-assed waltz begins to develop here in the center of the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Puke and rally!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yep!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to ask Caleb to waltz with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nott, I'm so proud of you. I'm so proud of you! So well done!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You were great out there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the midst of everything dying down, people are starting to get pretty sloshed, and the energy level, while still rather rowdy, some folks are starting to tap out for the night. Jester drags Caleb into the center of the tavern floor, takes hands, and Caleb, almost running out on autopilot, who does have some minor experience in the past. Go ahead and make a performance check with disadvantage, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's cocked. That's okay. With advantage, you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Disadvantage, because you're drunk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's very low. Very low. That is a five.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys eventually find yourselves to rest for the night, sleep. Long arduous morning comes to, as the sun comes up through the windows. The sound of distant, wandering roosters crowing at daybreak break you into consciousness. The hangovers are real. Beau's doing okay. There's a little bit of a hangover, but not too bad. Jester's totally sober. Yasha's sober.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No! No, that was a party foul!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I wouldn't touch that!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Was that inside of me? Did you touch inside me? Oh, fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for being part of my little experiment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, now what?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was just curious if I could pull liquor out of people. I'm going to go throw up on Fjord, excuse me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to find Fjord and make sure that however I throw up, it makes it so it looks like he did it to himself. (groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No! Not like that!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you leave Fjord in the bathroom?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god no. I bring him back in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys all make yourselves back to your rooms. As you check inside, Yasha is asleep in a corner, there with her sword at the side, ready. Curled up against her chest is Kiri, also asleep, bundled up, a little bustle of feathers.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She tried to shave her.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To your memory, they didn't. Shortly after the competition, tucking their tails, they went to find another place to drink and not have to be in the eyes of people that watched them lose.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should ask Irena if it happens often when these things go on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I thought you said you had something.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yes. I have a spell that can locate objects.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That cuts down on our detective work a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I could locate potentially a money purse.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you remember what my bag looked like? It matches my sash that you still have on your wand.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's on this wand, yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I almost forgot we tied that on! That was funny. Man, we were drunk.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can we bring the volume level down?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But it was only the five who competed who were robbed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was only the five who got stupid drunk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We were super drunk.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It could be because we won, and Blemmy's crew stole our money.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should find out if Blemmy's crew also got stolen from. Do we remember if Blemmy's crew hung around the tavern afterwards?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The food is being brought out to you slowly over the next 30 minutes or so. The crownsguard looks to you. He's human, a bushy, not really well-kept blond beard.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Of course I would.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll go check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we go look at the cart. Is it still there and protected?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You turn around and the cart is protected. There are crownsguard that patrol the main area. It's not as busy as it was last night, of course. It seems they come out in full force. A lot of them seem to sleep during the day and then come out at night to keep watch, because that's when a lot of things happen, but there are a few. There's one that's posted on the far corner. It looks like nobody's messed with the cart because it is out in the open.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should've left our money in the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me! Can I have a mi-- (hiccups) minute of your time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you referring to--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The crownsguard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you've exited the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm walking out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm sitting down to breakfast.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going back in the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me too.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Okay! I've been hoping they'd pay attention to this. Since the whole war's going on, I think it'd be quite useful. I've lost some good favor with folks in power, but I hope maybe this will turn my fortune around." He goes around, off to the shelf, and pulls this down, and a bow of some kind that's affixed to this large device. It looks like a crossbow, but it's got a heavy crank on one side, and it has all these extra grooves put in where it's easy to assemble a bolt in there. The actual shaft of it is made of this polished, treated wood, with metal casing where the grip is. It's like a half-clockwork, gear-based crank hand crossbow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you like it? How much is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, that one, it's a beautiful piece. I'll be happy to let it go for about three gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Three gold pieces. I think that's worth it, Kiri. Okay. Three gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's a whole smattering of cool contraptions, now that you're looking at the back, too. Some that look like they could be armaments, almost. Some that look like they're the insides of larger machine pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What's the thing you're most proud of?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ah, proud of, now? I have one thing I'm working on, if you want to see it."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, absolutely!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "They tell me. They keep it down there still. They've sealed it off and it's been there for the better part of two years and every now then, they like to remind me. I didn't mean anything bad by it. I was just doing my job and I thought I was very proud of my work. I didn't see the problems there. I was just trying to help."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Uh--" and Rissa's like, "Ah, it's-- you don't have to answer him. We can go find the boys." "It's all right." Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Come on. Shamone! Oh, yeah, that's a 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 20? You see his trepidation there, and he goes, "You look like you've got an honest face, so: I was working on a job here for the wardens of the Gearhold Prison down below. I was helping them and came up with, at their request, what would be a clockwork warden. Something that could help keep watch over some of the more dangerous and difficult inmates of the prison below. And I did it. I was very proud of it. On its first foray, it had some faults I didn't see coming, and we couldn't control it. It began to just destroy whatever was in its way."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you speaking of an automaton?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "More or less."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did it kill people?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: They both look at each other. "Well, originally, he used to be an ice sailor up north, by Ice Haven, but he retired ten years ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're making kids cry. This is amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ashton's like, "Come on! Get it together!" and kicks him and is like, "We're being completely honest. We saw nothing, we took nothing. We were just getting our rocks off freaking people out, and then we went back and got some drinks."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You don't know anyone else that has made a habit of doing this regularly, then?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Not personally, no. Most of them end up in the Gearhold."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should I pour this vial of acid on them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (screaming)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You might get it ready, Nott. I feel like we're not getting anywhere here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You know where Ol' Blemmy is from?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There's a pause, like the two workers at the forge look at each other and look at you guys and go (laughs). "I'm not going to lie, those boys are going to be pissing their pants for a week after that. That was enjoyable."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I want this one, particularly, working now. Why don't we stake their salary for the day? We take care of that, double it even. We need these two. We pay for the loss of their work today?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They nod. The gnome steps forward, takes the coin, if you present it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We don't have any.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's four gold, so that's three of them for the two gold. He goes, "Okay, you boys go get your work done here and we'll find some replacements, and you hope they aren't better than you are." The boys are like, "Yes! Yes, sir!" "Yes, sir!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I crack my staff on the floor and I say Get fucking moving, then!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They get up and grab their stuff and then dart out into the street.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Excellent.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should I shoot them down while they run?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Not yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The two forge workers--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She goes, "The Assembly Yard is down in the bottom half of the city, so it's say about an hour, hour and a half walk?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys wander outside?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: On the way out, can I just ask that gentleman Do you make any of those things that we've seen around here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Scissors?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Octopi?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (stammering) The pistols. Do you make those here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, no. We don't specialize in firearms. Those are all regulated by the Empire. Those are designed down in the Assembly Yard."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Thank you. Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Or that would be the Iron Lot is where that would be made.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Iron Lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, when we were in there, the shields and swords, they looked fairly standard, or were there some that were like "holy shit!"?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, they seemed pretty standard. I mean, well-made. Looks like a lot of places like this are in the process of developing weaponry, not just for home use, but probably sending it out for the field.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Rissa, how far are we from this Assembly Yard?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, that's that way, about 25 minutes to get to the actual Falls themselves, and you see all the little towers over there, those are where all the homes are assembled in that place."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Thank you. Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Or that would be the Iron Lot is where that would be made.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Iron Lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, when we were in there, the shields and swords, they looked fairly standard, or were there some that were like "holy shit!"?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, they seemed pretty standard. I mean, well-made. Looks like a lot of places like this are in the process of developing weaponry, not just for home use, but probably sending it out for the field.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Rissa, how far are we from this Assembly Yard?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She goes, "The Assembly Yard is down in the bottom half of the city, so it's say about an hour, hour and a half walk?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And just out of curiosity, the Silver Falls, the residential area, how far is that?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Avandra. It's been in the family for a long time. Every six months, the tithe collectors come through, and they noticed they'd forgotten to put away their idol worship. They locked them up. They've been in the Gearhold ever since."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've scrambled coins here and there over time, keep on by. Most everyone here only keeps a few coins in their pockets. Hence our surprise when this came about." The younger boy goes like, "Yeah, we don't expect this kind of a payload."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How old are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The older girl goes, "I'm Gail. I'm 17."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Gail!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The older boy goes, "I'm Austin." The little boy with longer curly hair goes, "I'm Jude." The older girl points to the little girl and goes, "That's Layla."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You said you haven't had your parents around for a while now? What happened to them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all look at each other, and the older boy sits forward and goes, "Well, they got arrested."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Doing what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Got arrested for idol worship. False worship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Here? In Hupperdook?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What god were they worshiping?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was it the Traveler?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No. Changebringer."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Changebringer?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You know about those. Those of you who've been in the Empire, part of living in the Empire is about every six months or so, there's a tax man, a tithe collector that goes through, usually with a couple crownsguard. You recognize them by black cloak with gold trim, usually dark clothing. They go building to building, business to business, inspect ledgers, ask about their business, look at the means in which they're living, and then make an estimation of what they owe. They then collect the money and move on their way. But you know this from your studies, this is also used as a means of inspecting for any illegal activity and anything that goes against the Crown. Often a lot of people get arrested for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The older girl goes, "I'm Gail. I'm 17."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Gail!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all look at each other, and the older boy sits forward and goes, "Well, they got arrested."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Doing what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Got arrested for idol worship. False worship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Here? In Hupperdook?

preceding_turns.text: What god were they worshiping?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was it the Traveler?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No. Changebringer."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Changebringer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Avandra. It's been in the family for a long time. Every six months, the tithe collectors come through, and they noticed they'd forgotten to put away their idol worship. They locked them up. They've been in the Gearhold ever since."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tithe collectors?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Anytime now, really. It's been six months. But we ain't much of a business at this time."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, no, I understand." Gail says, "We understand, sir! We've learned our lesson!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

raw_text: Okay. Midday, you make your way to the Pentamarket, to the very vibrant center area of the marketplace there. There's a number of intersecting pathways that help divide between all the various carts and tents that open up. At this point, I'd say it's maybe late morning or so. A lot of the curled up curtains in the front are being affixed to the top or have been for a while. The smells of fresh baked goods, cooking stews, and various other treats and snacks that are being presented as part of the shopping experience catch your nostrils as you move through. You hear music playing in the distance. For all the chaos that life has thrown you in the past 30 days, it feels nice to step into a place that's so filled with life and lack of understanding of how dark the world is at the fringes of this empire, both with the war going on and some of the things you've encountered. It's nice to step into this space and you catch yourself instinctually smiling and finding it infectious to brighten your mood. You turn to see the darkened green wood with gold accoutrements that marks the exterior of The Invulnerable Vagrant. The front door is closed, but appears to be not locked for any particular reason.

preceding_turns.text: And we sit there while she pulls out the entire bag.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That would be Nicodranas. Nicodranas from Zadash, it's a little over a week to get there, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Yeah. The previous events have rocked Fjord a little bit. Are there any bars or taverns in Zadash that may house frequent sailors or people of trade, merchants?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sailors, not so much. The Empire is landlocked aside from the very far north, and those waters are in the frigid depths. It's a lot of ice floes and treacherous water up there. So there is very little passage up north. The Menagerie Coast, as you know, is where all of the port towns are, and that's the relationship between the Empire and The Menagerie Coast. That agreement is based on the fact that, essentially, the Menagerie Coast holds all the long-term imports and exports in the palm of their hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I know that I've made that trip. How far is it from Zadash back to the northernmost part of the Menagerie Coast?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You make it as far south as Trostenwald before the frustration wells up inside you. It's been a series of dead ends. You've asked around and listened, and anyone who would've crossed paths with some of the shipping lanes that you're familiar with and that Sabian was familiar with, none of them are passing this far north. You assume that to continue this, you would have to go to the Menagerie Coast, and that would involve a thorough passage through Nicodranas and the various wharf taverns and seeing what old connections you might be able to dredge up in that time, but that's going to take you a little while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would be Nicodranas. Nicodranas from Zadash, it's a little over a week to get there, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) I think Fjord's going to take a steed and start heading south by himself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What? He's going on walkabout?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's going on a walkabout.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: While he heads south, I'd like to adopt various disguises as I stop in inns, taverns, places of trade, and carefully hear if anyone is mentioning the name "Sabian," if people are talking about ships, and try and casually make my way further south, knowing that I have an amount of time to get back north. I'd like to try to make my way down and see what I can hear and find out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Make three successive investigation checks for me, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 12, nine, and a six.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. We'll get to that. First, you guys make your way to the archive and you enter there, and the following morning Caleb is very tired-looking, but also hard to tell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Cat Frumpkin has returned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There is only one Frumpkin. I stayed up all night doing all of those things because they're very time-intensive and would have went to find Beauregard, very tired and ready to go, to stay at the library as long as I am able. She said a number of days, and when those days are up, the thing that I would do is spend as much time that is left with the-- I would borrow Jester's haversack, if she would let me, and spend time with the dodecahedron.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, she's been sent to assess political threats beyond the eastern border. Been gone for about 30 days and is said to return in the next 45 before we send anyone after her. But I'm sure she's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Making your way into the archive, it's fairly bustling in comparison to what you know. It's either dead or there's a handful of people going through it. Today it's a handful of people going through. You're like, "Oh wow, there's four people in here researching right now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No one ever cares about books this much. It's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Within a short time-- what are you looking to do, Beauregard?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I ask around and see if Dairon is there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One of the monks goes, "Let me go inquire. I'll be right back," and wanders up the stairs. You see them descend the stairs with another figure. Gray robes with blue lining, hood pulled back, and there you see Zeenoth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He steps down and goes, "Beauregard. I wasn't aware you had returned."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hi.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hi. Dairon, unfortunately, is still out on the front lines."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. Tubo brings you to a chamber you haven't been in in a while, which is the formal training grounds beneath the archive. There is a set of double doors in the far end that blend in mostly with the dark wood that a lot of the bookcases are crafted out of. It leads you down a set of stairs into what is essentially a training pit with very heavily packed sand and dirt, and there are a number of wooden poles that emerge from beneath that have all sorts of skewed extensions. This is where a lot of forms are practiced and a lot of defensive maneuvers are utilized. You were brought through in this area for training in waves time and again, but it's been a while since you've been down here, since you've been abroad, and there's no other people there training currently. Tubo brings you down. "Very well. Show me what you've got." For the sake of brevity--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is he an expositor?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hmm. Yeah, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. I shall return. Good seeing you again."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth's patient smile fades as he turns. Within a short time, you see coming from down the same staircase, Tubo, his arms folded in front of him as he descends and turns toward you. "So you are Beauregard, correct?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And you're looking for training while your expositor is away, correct?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Follow me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Also, I got this guy with me. Can he get a room and a library pass?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth, who's a little bit behind, goes, "I'll accompany him in his reading, if that's all right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth, Caleb. Caleb, Zeenoth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've met, in Trostenwald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hallo.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hello. Please, lead the way to where you'd wish to read."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, thank you. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yes. Roll up a new character. Tubo splatters you across the wall. You spar for a bit, showing him the limits of your capabilities, and he's deftly, with a smile on his face, dodging, parrying. You get a few good hits in and he backs away. "Very good," and then begins to overwhelm you with his assault, but never enough to inflict pain, just enough to throw you off your guard until a moment he turns around and puts his hands up into a cross once more and goes, "Four days. I need four days with you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You die.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "That you are an apt pupil. Now, our time is done. I have things to do. And four days with you has been more than enough."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And one more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Spend another ki point. That's two ki points now. You get to roll to reduce the third one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that one does no damage. You catch one arrow out of the air. It just barely nicks your shoulder. The other one you catch just as it scrapes past the side of your arm. The third one you catch in the middle of the air with no impact. In the blink of an eye, you've deflected all three arrows shot at the same time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn to Tubo and I go  Did you see that?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I did. And with that, your training is complete."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I don't get to kill those guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No!" He hands the shortbow to the other one and they all head back upstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, I'm sure you're really lovely people.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Impressive. Dairon was not wrong about you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what has-- Dairon's been talking about me? What has she been saying?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There's nothing about beacons, nothing about reincarnation. However, it does speak of the dynasty worshiping something other, something that is new and dangerous as far as the Cobalt Soul is concerned, with what little research they have. They refer to it as "The Luxon" and seek to bring all under its burning banner. They've rallied to the surface of Ghor Dranas, casting the skies in eternal midnight that spreads with their dominion. They've begun taking the ravenous hordes of beast-men and terrors of the Xhorhasian wastes, and indoctrinate them under this new false god. You also learn that a number of people, mostly children of adolescent age, have been found abducted in the night by the Kryn on occasion, though the number has steeply grown this past generation, years after the first open conflicts with the Kryn began. The last thing you discover is the leader of the dynasty is known as Empress Leylas Kryn. She is called an "umavi" by them, though the word's meaning is unknown by current research. Accounts of the Empress speak of her as a powerful and dangerous warrior on the battlefield. And well over 600 years old or more, as records date back that far since she arrived in local history. That's what you've discovered about the Kryn Dynasty and Xhorhas.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You see? You know it, too. That means you looked at me because you knew.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Of course. As soon as I read the word I was like-- Without fail.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pray to the great god titty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We missed you. But for those who were able to move past the boundaries to research more of what little is known of the Kryn Dynasty, there is little in iconography under Lolth or Tharizdun. And what remains of that is ancient and crumbling and largely ignored by the society.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I find any mention of these beacons-- he mentioned beacons, plural-- and reincarnation or multiple lives?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The things you discover, from what's available to you in the archive: time is a constant, the motion moving forward unyielding. Some powerful arcane influences can accelerate or slow the rate at which time passes locally, even stop it. There are some stories from the Age of Arcanum that speak of the greatest of sages experimenting with reversing the forward momentum of time, and supposedly with some success. But the process apparently was so taxing and the energies required so extensive that it nearly killed those who passed through this natural flow. That's as much as you can pick up on the specific research involving that shift of time. They're more footnotes and like a, "We don't mess with that because those who tried have mostly been disintegrated or destroyed in the process."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I spend a day reading about that. I will spend every remaining day reading about-- I was rushed the first time I came here, so I'm going to spend every day left reading about the weave of time and any mortals' attempts to control it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The weave of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's some restricted reading bullshit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yes, but they existed close to 1500 years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nearly killed. Does it talk about any of the people who were nearly killed but weren't?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No, it's near the crossguard, in the center of the hilt. When you look at it, it doesn't seem to move or shift or pay any attention. Practicing the next few days and trying some things out, you focus some of your arcane gifts towards an object, casting Witch Bolt towards a nearby rock or low, dried, and forgotten trunk of a tree. At the moment the incantation begins, the eye shifts to look towards the target of the spell. The spell explodes and you look back at the hilt, and it's already reverted back to where it was before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I was like, I feel like I came up-- Oh, there's more shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I was like, are you sure that's all you want to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. We can just say on the way back from those visits, at night when I made camp, I want to summon the falchion and spend more time with it. Examine it, specifically the eye, if it has that sort of-- in the letter, it looked like there was a gash into the sphere itself. Does it represent that, is it smooth? Does it move?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The eye, where it sits in the hilt-- and it only sits on one side, it's not both sides that contain the eye, because there aren't two pupils. There is a singular eye that is embedded in it, that is looking out what would essentially be towards the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it near the crossguard, or is it near the pommel?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Sure, all right. So you make your way to The Evening Nip. By the time you've returned, it's been the better part of the two-week period of time, and you guys are also generally worried the fact that Fjord just vanished during this time period.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it near the crossguard, or is it near the pommel?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's near the crossguard, in the center of the hilt. When you look at it, it doesn't seem to move or shift or pay any attention. Practicing the next few days and trying some things out, you focus some of your arcane gifts towards an object, casting Witch Bolt towards a nearby rock or low, dried, and forgotten trunk of a tree. At the moment the incantation begins, the eye shifts to look towards the target of the spell. The spell explodes and you look back at the hilt, and it's already reverted back to where it was before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Super fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But it did seem to react to when the spell was cast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Creepy. What the hell does that mean? Okay. When I get back, could I make my way to The Gentleman for just a quick convo?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You make your way to the subterranean basement of The Evening Nip. The Gentleman is not currently present, but upon asking around, and you wait for about 20 minutes or so, he does return from the storage room to the side where you had previously been led down and through the Underworks. As he comes in, he sees you and sits on the other side of the table, throwing his leg over one side and sitting at a very comfortable and casual way across from you. "Fjord, I'm being told that you're looking for me."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Super fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But it did seem to react to when the spell was cast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Creepy. What the hell does that mean? Okay. When I get back, could I make my way to The Gentleman for just a quick convo?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, all right. So you make your way to The Evening Nip. By the time you've returned, it's been the better part of the two-week period of time, and you guys are also generally worried the fact that Fjord just vanished during this time period.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He left while we were shopping.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Big surprise.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Travis is shouting.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You guys are going to shop, ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (poof sound)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Spooky exit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "The jewel of the coast. I've been a number of times, though it's been years since I've been able to leave my work here alone. So if you're looking to expand your portfolio beyond Zadash, that is commendable. The one contact I could recommend in Port Damali would be Father Dwondaff Pierce."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have some reaches in places, yes. That's quite a scope you're requesting. I would say it's a good bet that most criminal elements you would encounter there are not at my network, except for a handful. It might be more useful to you to know the ones to trust."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's where I was going with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay. Port Damali, you say?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's where I'm from.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Really?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Indeed.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It is all right. I have started a new life here, and it has been serving me well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We of the Task Takers, we were undergoing a ritual that he had orchestrated with an ally of his, a woman of magical renown. He said that this was something that was meant to lead us to this city he promised, a place where we could learn of a great many powers and secrets. We believed him. He was very charismatic in his cruelty at times. But he did not survive."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And then you saw him here for the first time in quite a while, yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two years.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two years? Two years. Are you-- and forgive me if this is too personal-- are you expecting to see him again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I wasn't expecting to see him this last time. I feel like whatever grace brought him back to us, maybe it was your carelessness that took him from us again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Indeed, maybe it was. You have my apologies and my condolences.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He's there most of the day, and in the evening he appears to go nap somewhere within the Platinum House.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (snoring) (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But yeah, the midday stakeout appears to go well. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How often does he come back?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys approach in the evening, together? Are you disguising yourself as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Your Anarchist's Cookbook.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My Anarchist's Cookbook, a couple brochures for the Traveler, and I steal Cad's straw hat that he bought-- sorry-- and, yeah, we're going to make the Platinum Dragon look like a really cool farmer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay. So we're going to mess with the statue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's what I do. Is it stupid or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What if someone comes in? We kill everyone in the place?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, if somebody comes in, you need to distract them, okay? Don't let anybody see me, because this might take a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So I'm the distraction and the lookout.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're the distraction. I also Invoke Duplicity to where it looks like I'm still there on the outside and I can also be hanging out with you so you don't look like a random person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What do you disguise yourself to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The old woman that was in the other, yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. Jester, now taking the elderly form of a silver-scaled, very well-respected, Herald of the House Kumas. You approach the temple a few hours after sundown, to where the interest in worship tends to fade as folks go back to home. It's mostly just a few caretakers that watch it through the night and some lost souls that have found their way there in the evening and they need guidance.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay. So we're going to mess with the statue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's what I do. Is it stupid or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, no. You know what I should disguise myself as? The sermon guy. Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It works out okay. Having all spent the majority of these two weeks handling your business, wandering through Zadash and beyond, returning, what do you wish to do? You guys have regathered in the Leaky Tap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. Before we get back into this, we have our winner of the giveaway, of the fantastic Deven Rue-carved cherry wood dice tower system. The winner is Mellydew. Congratulations. We'll get your info and get this sent out to you ASAP, so thank you for being a part. Laura will be joining us shortly; she's finishing feeding the baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, feed the baby, give it life.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Feed me!
